Me: "Did you eat today?"

Mom: "Yes."

Me: "What did you eat?"

Mom: "Food."

Me: "How are you feeling?"

Mom: "Fine."

Me: "..."

Me: "Are you busy or something?"

Mom: "No."

Me: "Then why are your answers only two words long?"

Mom: "I'm waiting for you to get to the point."

Me: "The point is I miss you."

Mom (three-second pause): "...That's so cheesy."

Mom (three more seconds): "Same here."

Then she hangs up immediately.

Happy Mother's Day to every mom who answers in two words and hangs up the second things get emotional.
en.editorNote: Two-word answers, then a 'same here,' then an instant hang-up — the universal mom-phone format. The three-second pauses are the love language. Works as a Mother's Day caption that every adult kid recognizes instantly
variations: Short version: "The three seconds between Mom saying 'same here' and hanging up the phone is the most romantic moment of her life."

items.sourceNote: 母親節幽默祝福語 trope: 媽媽嘴上叫你別買禮物 — rewritten as original family scene

zh-tw.content:
母親節前一週。

我：「媽，今年想要什麼禮物？」

媽：「不用啦，你錢留著自己用。」

我：「真的不用？」

媽：「真的不用。」

我：「好，那我就不買了。」

媽（停三秒）：「⋯⋯欸你怎麼這麼聽話。」

我：「妳剛剛叫我不要買的。」

媽：「我那是客氣。」

我：「那妳到底要什麼？」

媽：「你自己看著辦。」

我：「⋯⋯妳能不能講清楚？」

媽：「以前我都自己看著辦，把你拉拔長大。」

我安靜地打開購物 App。

母親節快樂，獻給所有「不用啦」翻譯起來其實是「你最好給我準備好」的媽媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽說「不用」實際上是滿分試題——這是亞洲家庭通用語言。傳到家族群組或當母親節限動文案，每個被媽媽考過的小孩都會默默點頭
variations: 短版：「媽媽說『不用啦』的時候，是這輩子最危險的三個字。」

en.content:
Week before Mother's Day.

Me: "Mom, what do you want this year?"

Mom: "Nothing. Save your money."

Me: "Really nothing?"

Mom: "Really nothing."

Me: "Okay, I won't get anything then."

Mom (three-second pause): "...Wow. Since when do you listen to me."

Me: "You just told me not to."

Mom: "I was being polite."

Me: "So what do you actually want?"

Mom: "Figure it out yourself."

Me: "...Can you give me a hint?"

Mom: "I figured out how to raise you. You can figure out a present."

I quietly open the shopping app.

Happy Mother's Day to every mom whose 'don't get me anything' translates directly to 'you'd better get me something good.'
en.editorNote: Mom saying 'don't get me anything' is a pop quiz, not a statement. Every adult kid recognizes this trap. Works as a Mother's Day caption that lands instantly with anyone who's been here
variations: Short version: "'Don't get me anything' is the most dangerous sentence Mom will ever say to you."

items.sourceNote: 粽子諧音祝福「包中」+ 考試 trope from 端午祝福語 lists; rewritten with original dialogue and punchline

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一天，阿姨傳了一顆粽子表情符號到家族群組。

阿姨：「祝大家端午節包中、包高中、包好運！」

我表妹：「謝謝阿姨，我下週學測。」

阿姨：「那妳一定包中！」

我表妹：「真的嗎？」

阿姨：「真的，包中考完之後一定會有結果。」

我表妹：「⋯⋯這跟沒講有什麼差別？」

阿姨：「妳要正面思考。」

我表妹：「妳這叫廢話文學。」

阿姨：「廢話也是話。」

群組安靜了五分鐘。

端午節快樂，獻給所有用粽子當護身符的考生，跟所有以為「包中」是一種祝福的阿姨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「包中」=「包粽」諧音是端午經典梗，搭配阿姨的廢話文學完美收尾。考季前後傳到家族群組或學生群效果加倍
variations: 短版：「端午節吃粽子包中，沒中至少包飽。」

en.content:
Day before Dragon Boat Festival. Auntie posts a zongzi emoji in the family group chat.

Auntie: "May everyone have a wrapped-up, locked-in, lucky holiday!"

My cousin: "Thanks Auntie, I have a big exam next week."

Auntie: "Then you'll definitely pass!"

My cousin: "Really?"

Auntie: "Really. After you take the exam, you'll definitely have a result."

My cousin: "...How is that different from saying nothing?"

Auntie: "Think positive."

My cousin: "That's not positive, that's just empty words."

Auntie: "Empty words are still words."

The group chat goes silent for five minutes.

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every student treating zongzi like a good-luck charm, and every auntie who thinks 'you'll have a result' counts as a blessing.
en.editorNote: The Chinese pun 'bao zhong' (wrapping zongzi) sounds exactly like 'guaranteed pass,' so families bless students with zongzi during exam season. Auntie's empty-words logic is the universal family group chat experience
variations: Short version: "Eat zongzi during Dragon Boat Festival. If you don't pass the exam, at least you pass on snacks."

items.sourceNote: 屈原投江典故與現代職場壓力 mash-up trope; rewritten as original short joke

zh-tw.content:
我跟同事說：「下週端午節耶，我們可以放假了。」

同事看著電腦螢幕：「不能放，老闆說專案要趕。」

我：「可是政府公告放假。」

同事：「老闆比政府大。」

我：「⋯⋯」

我：「你知道屈原為什麼投江嗎？」

同事：「為什麼？」

我：「因為他不想加班。」

同事沉默三秒。

同事：「那我等等下班路過河邊，幫他撒一顆粽子。」

我：「順便幫我也撒一顆。」

同事：「你為什麼？」

我：「我也快不想加班了。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「放假是別人家的事」的台灣上班族，記得粽子要冰起來，肝要顧好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 屈原+加班的台式自嘲組合，端午連假前傳到同事群或社群文案剛剛好，所有被工作綁住的人都會苦笑
variations: 短版：「屈原投江是因為他想休假，我加班是因為我不敢。」

en.content:
I tell my coworker: "Dragon Boat Festival is next week — we get a day off."

Coworker, eyes on screen: "Nope. Boss says we're crunching."

Me: "But the government declared it a holiday."

Coworker: "Boss outranks the government."

Me: "..."

Me: "You know why Qu Yuan jumped in the river, right?"

Coworker: "Why?"

Me: "He didn't want to work overtime."

Coworker pauses for three seconds.

Coworker: "I'll throw a rice dumpling in the river on my way home for him."

Me: "Throw one in for me too."

Coworker: "Why you?"

Me: "I'm getting close to the same decision."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every office worker whose 'public holiday' is just a calendar suggestion. Refrigerate the zongzi. Protect the liver.
en.editorNote: Qu Yuan was an ancient poet who drowned himself in protest — Dragon Boat Festival commemorates him. The 'he was avoiding overtime' angle is dark Taiwanese office humor that hits anyone whose holiday got eaten by a deadline
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan jumped in the river because he wanted time off. I work overtime because I'm too scared to."

items.sourceNote: Memorial Day BBQ + grill humor classic American format; rewritten as original short scene with new punchline

zh-tw.content:
美國 Memorial Day 連假，我朋友請我去他家後院烤肉。

朋友站在烤肉架前，圍裙上寫著「Grill Master」。

他：「You came to the right place. 我烤肉技術全街最強。」

二十分鐘後。

他：「⋯⋯你覺得這塊肉是熟了還是焦了？」

我：「兩個。」

他：「兩個？」

我：「外面焦，裡面生。」

他：「那這叫什麼？」

我：「叫生焦兩相宜。」

他默默把那塊肉藏到盤子最底下。

他：「等等沒人發現的話，這塊就當作貢品獻給烤肉之神。」

我：「烤肉之神會生氣。」

他：「為什麼？」

我：「祂收到的是焦炭。」

Memorial Day 快樂，獻給所有圍裙寫 Grill Master 但烤出炭烤遺照的美國爸爸們。
zh-tw.editorNote: Memorial Day 連假是美國夏天烤肉開季——「Grill Master」圍裙加翻車烤肉是美式家庭經典場景，台灣人看到應該也會心一笑（因為我們爸爸過年烤肉也是同一齣）
variations: 短版：「Memorial Day 烤肉的真相是：圍裙寫 Grill Master，肉烤成 Grill Disaster。」

en.content:
Memorial Day weekend. My friend invites me to his backyard cookout.

He's standing at the grill in an apron that says "Grill Master."

Him: "You came to the right place. Best grill skills on the block."

Twenty minutes later.

Him: "...Would you say this piece is done, or burnt?"

Me: "Both."

Him: "Both?"

Me: "Burnt outside, raw inside."

Him: "What do you call that?"

Me: "A speedrun of food poisoning."

He quietly buries that piece at the bottom of the platter.

Him: "If nobody notices, it's an offering to the grill gods."

Me: "The grill gods are gonna be mad."

Him: "Why?"

Me: "You sacrificed them charcoal."

Happy Memorial Day to every dad whose apron says 'Grill Master' and whose burgers say 'call poison control.'
en.editorNote: Memorial Day weekend kicks off American grilling season. The 'Grill Master apron + disaster results' combo is the universal backyard BBQ trope — works as a holiday social caption for anyone whose dad has overcooked the kickoff cookout
variations: Short version: "Memorial Day weekend truth: the apron says Grill Master, the burgers say Grill Disaster."

items.sourceNote: 母親節「我把妳當朋友」反差梗 from 母親節梗圖; rewritten as original mother-daughter dialogue

zh-tw.content:
母親節，我請媽媽吃飯。

我：「媽，我都把妳當朋友。」

媽：「真的？」

我：「真的。」

媽：「那我們去酒吧喝一杯。」

我：「⋯⋯妳明天不是要早起？」

媽：「朋友才不會管彼此這種事。」

我：「那我們去夜店。」

媽：「我膝蓋不行。」

我：「妳剛剛說朋友不管這種事。」

媽：「朋友也要互相體諒。」

我：「⋯⋯」

我：「所以妳到底是不是我朋友？」

媽：「我是妳媽。妳剛剛說的。」

我：「我說我把妳『當』朋友。」

媽：「我把妳『當』乖小孩。」

我安靜地把帳單收回來自己付。

母親節快樂，獻給所有「我們是朋友」「但我還是妳媽」雙開模式的台灣媽媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「我把妳當朋友」是母親節常見子女台詞，但媽媽永遠有大絕。母親節限動或家族群組傳這個會被各種「我媽也這樣」洗版
variations: 短版：「跟媽媽說『我把妳當朋友』之前，先想清楚妳付不付得起朋友的開銷。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. I take Mom out to dinner.

Me: "Mom, I think of you as a friend."

Mom: "Really?"

Me: "Really."

Mom: "Then let's go grab drinks at a bar."

Me: "...Don't you have to be up early?"

Mom: "Friends don't worry about that kind of thing."

Me: "Okay, then let's go clubbing."

Mom: "My knees can't handle it."

Me: "You just said friends don't worry about that kind of thing."

Mom: "Friends also need to be considerate."

Me: "..."

Me: "So are you my friend or not?"

Mom: "I'm your mother. You said so yourself."

Me: "I said I think of you as a friend."

Mom: "I think of you as a well-behaved child."

I quietly take the bill back and pay it myself.

Happy Mother's Day to every mom who runs 'we're friends' and 'I'm still your mother' on the same operating system.

Dual-boot. No reboot needed.
en.editorNote: 'I think of you as a friend' is the classic Mother's Day kid line. Mom always has a counter. Lands as a Mother's Day caption with anyone who has tried — and immediately regretted — the friend-zone speech with their mother
variations: Short version: "Before telling Mom 'I see you as a friend,' check whether you can afford a friend's bar tab."

items.sourceNote: 「running late is mom's cardio」+「clean house = nothing」classic Mother's Day humor; rewritten as original Taiwanese family scene

zh-tw.content:
母親節前一週，我問媽媽想要什麼禮物。

媽：「我什麼都不缺。」

我：「真的什麼都不要？」

媽：「真的。」

我：「那我就不買了喔。」

媽：「⋯⋯你是真的不買嗎？」

我：「你剛剛說什麼都不缺。」

媽：「我是說禮物，但你心意要有。」

我：「那心意長什麼樣子？」

媽：「心意長得像那台你上次在百貨公司看到，我多看兩眼的果汁機。」

我：「⋯⋯」

我：「所以你不缺什麼，但缺一台果汁機？」

媽：「對。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「我什麼都不要」翻譯成「我已經選好了你只要刷卡」的台灣媽媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽說「不要禮物」其實是「我已經幫你選好了」——母親節前一週傳到家族群組或社群，所有當過孩子的人都會點頭如搗蒜。配上百貨公司家電廣告效果加倍
variations: 短版：「媽媽說『什麼都不要』的時候，請打開錢包，因為下一句會很貴。」

en.content:
A week before Mother's Day, I ask Mom what she wants.

Mom: "I don't need anything."

Me: "Really, nothing?"

Mom: "Really."

Me: "Okay, I won't buy anything then."

Mom: "...You're actually not buying anything?"

Me: "You just said you don't need anything."

Mom: "I meant gifts. But the thought has to be there."

Me: "What does the thought look like?"

Mom: "The thought looks exactly like that blender we saw at the department store last month. The one I glanced at twice."

Me: "..."

Me: "So you don't need anything, but you do need a blender?"

Mom: "Correct."

Happy Mother's Day to every mom whose 'I don't want anything' translates to 'I've already picked it out, just bring your credit card.'
en.editorNote: When Mom says 'I don't need anything,' she has already picked the gift. Universal kid experience — works as a Mother's Day caption the week before the holiday, especially with a department store photo
variations: Short version: "When Mom says 'I don't need anything,' open your wallet. The next sentence will be expensive."

items.sourceNote: 母親節「我請客」反差梗 from 母親節幽默祝福語; rewritten as original restaurant dialogue

zh-tw.content:
母親節，我帶媽媽去高級日式料理。

我：「媽，今天我請客，你想點什麼就點什麼。」

媽看了一下菜單，眉頭一皺。

媽：「松阪豬一份要八百？」

我：「沒關係，你喜歡就點。」

媽：「鵝肝壽司一貫要兩百？」

我：「沒關係，今天是母親節。」

媽：「⋯⋯」

媽：「服務生，我們要兩碗烏龍麵就好。」

我：「媽，我說我請客。」

媽：「我知道，但你的錢還是我的錢。」

我：「⋯⋯這是我自己賺的。」

媽：「那也是我生的。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「孩子請客但比我還省」、最後自己默默把帳單接過去的台灣媽媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「孩子賺的錢媽媽看得最緊」是台灣媽媽宇宙真理。母親節限動或請媽媽吃飯前傳給朋友——所有人都會回「我媽也是這樣」
variations: 短版：「媽，今天我請客。」「我知道，所以你更要點便宜的。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. I take Mom to a fancy Japanese restaurant.

Me: "Mom, my treat. Order whatever you want."

Mom scans the menu and frowns.

Mom: "Pork jowl is twenty-five dollars?"

Me: "It's fine, get it if you like it."

Mom: "Foie gras sushi is six dollars per piece?"

Me: "It's fine, it's Mother's Day."

Mom: "..."

Mom: "Excuse me — we'll just have two bowls of udon."

Me: "Mom, I said I'm paying."

Mom: "I know. But your money is still my money."

Me: "...I earned this myself."

Mom: "And I made you myself."

Happy Mother's Day to every mom who guards her kid's wallet harder than her own — and then quietly pays the bill anyway.
en.editorNote: When the kid pays, Mom orders cheaper. Universal mom logic. Works as a Mother's Day caption right before or after taking Mom out to dinner
variations: Short version: "Me: 'Mom, my treat.' Mom: 'I know, so order something cheap.'"

items.sourceNote: 粽子諧音梗 + 健身/減肥 trope from 端午節笑話 thread; rewritten as original gym scene

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一週，我去健身房。

教練：「最近吃得怎麼樣？」

我：「還可以，蛋白質有控制。」

教練：「下週端午節，你打算吃幾顆粽子？」

我：「⋯⋯一顆。」

教練：「真的？」

我：「兩顆。」

教練：「真的？」

我：「五顆。我家阿嬤會包。」

教練嘆了一口氣。

教練：「那我先幫你預約一下下個月的課程。」

我：「為什麼？」

教練：「叫做『粽量訓練』。」

我：「⋯⋯」

我：「教練你這個諧音梗也太冷。」

教練：「冷沒關係，你下個月會很熱。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「明明知道會胖還是會吃」的台灣健身仔，記得粽子要冰起來，等你下次練腿日再吃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽量訓練」=「重量訓練」，端午節+健身房自我安慰的台式諧音梗——傳給有在練的朋友、或自己端午節前發限動標註教練都很適合
variations: 短版：「端午節後的健身房菜單只有一個，叫做粽量訓練。」

en.content:
A week before Dragon Boat Festival, I'm at the gym.

Trainer: "How's your diet been?"

Me: "Decent. Protein on track."

Trainer: "Next week is Dragon Boat Festival. How many zongzi are you eating?"

Me: "...One."

Trainer: "Really?"

Me: "Two."

Trainer: "Really?"

Me: "Five. My grandma is wrapping them."

Trainer sighs.

Trainer: "Let me book you in for some extra sessions next month."

Me: "Why?"

Trainer: "Call it dumpling-weight training."

Me: "..."

Me: "That pun is rough, coach."

Trainer: "The pun is cold, but next month you'll be sweating."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every gym rat who knows they'll regret it but eats five zongzi anyway. Refrigerate the rest. Save them for leg day.
en.editorNote: Zongzi (sticky rice dumplings) are calorie bombs — a single one runs 400-600 kcal. The 'I told my trainer I'd eat one but it'll be five' arc is the universal gym-goer holiday experience. Tag your coach for maximum guilt
variations: Short version: "The only post-Dragon-Boat-Festival workout is dumpling-weight training."

items.sourceNote: 「mom's coffee dependency」classic Mother's Day humor; rewritten as original morning kitchen scene

zh-tw.content:
母親節早上，我打算早起準備早餐給媽媽驚喜。

我七點走進廚房。

媽媽已經在廚房了，手裡拿著一杯咖啡。

我：「⋯⋯媽，你怎麼這麼早？」

媽：「我每天都這個時間。」

我：「今天是母親節，你不是應該休息？」

媽：「我休息全家就要餓肚子。」

我：「我做早餐給你。」

媽：「你會做？」

我：「⋯⋯我可以學。」

媽喝了一口咖啡，看著我。

媽：「兒子，你知道母親節最好的禮物是什麼嗎？」

我：「什麼？」

媽：「不要進廚房弄亂，讓我安靜喝完這杯咖啡。」

我：「⋯⋯」

我退出廚房。

媽（背景音）：「順便把昨天你丟在沙發上的衣服收一收。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「孩子的孝心比廚房災難更可怕」的台灣媽媽，咖啡記得喝熱的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 孩子想表現孝心，但媽媽更想要清靜——母親節最寫實的家庭場景。傳給家族群組或母親節限動，所有當過媽媽的人都會大笑說對對對
variations: 短版：「媽，母親節我幫你做早餐。」「兒子，你的孝心比火災更可怕。」

en.content:
Mother's Day morning. I plan to surprise Mom with breakfast.

I walk into the kitchen at seven.

Mom is already there, holding a coffee.

Me: "...Mom, why are you up so early?"

Mom: "I'm up at this time every day."

Me: "It's Mother's Day, shouldn't you be resting?"

Mom: "If I rest, the whole family starves."

Me: "I'll make you breakfast."

Mom: "You can cook?"

Me: "...I can learn."

Mom takes a sip and looks at me.

Mom: "Sweetie, you know what the best Mother's Day gift is?"

Me: "What?"

Mom: "Stay out of my kitchen and let me finish this coffee in peace."

Me: "..."

I back out of the kitchen.

Mom (calling after me): "And pick up the clothes you dumped on the couch last night."

Happy Mother's Day to every mom whose kid's helpfulness is scarier than a kitchen fire. Drink the coffee while it's hot.
en.editorNote: Kids want to show love. Moms want silence. The 'best gift is staying out of my kitchen' line is the universal mom truth. Works as a Mother's Day caption for anyone whose attempt to help has set off a smoke alarm
variations: Short version: "Me: 'Mom, I'll make breakfast.' Mom: 'Sweetie, your helpfulness is scarier than a house fire.'"

items.sourceNote: 端午節龍舟比賽 + 公司團建 humor concept from memes.tw 端午節梗圖集; rewritten as original office team-building scene

zh-tw.content:
公司宣布今年端午節要辦龍舟團建。

主管：「划龍舟可以增進團隊凝聚力。」

阿明舉手：「請問是上班時間嗎？」

主管：「週六早上。」

阿明：「那有加班費嗎？」

主管：「沒有，這是團隊建設活動。」

阿明：「請問下雨會取消嗎？」

主管：「不會。」

阿明：「請問可以不參加嗎？」

主管：「可以，但我會記住。」

阿明默默放下手。

當天划龍舟比賽，阿明的隊伍最後一名。

阿明的座右銘：「主管說划快，我就划慢；主管說團結，我就先溺水。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「龍舟團建是公司省錢辦活動的最佳藉口」的台灣社畜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 公司用「文化傳承」當包裝強迫週末團建，是社畜共同陰影——傳給那個下週要被迫去划船的同事，配上他無奈的表情包效果最好
variations: 短版：「公司：龍舟團建增進向心力。員工：那能不能直接給我加班費，我可以自己在家增進。」

en.content:
Company announces a dragon boat team-building event for the holiday.

Manager: "Rowing builds team cohesion."

Ming raises his hand: "Is this on company time?"

Manager: "Saturday morning."

Ming: "Overtime pay?"

Manager: "No, it's team building."

Ming: "Cancelled if it rains?"

Manager: "No."

Ming: "Can I skip?"

Manager: "Yes. But I'll remember."

Ming silently puts his hand down.

Day of the race, Ming's team finishes last.

Ming's life philosophy: "Boss says row fast, I row slow. Boss says team unity, I drown first."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every office worker whose company disguised a free Saturday with a paddle in their hand as 'cultural heritage.'
en.editorNote: Mandatory weekend team-building disguised as cultural celebration — universal corporate trauma. Send to the friend dragged into a Saturday dragon boat race against their will
variations: Short version: "Company: dragon boat building team spirit. Employee: just give me overtime pay, I'll build spirit at home."

items.sourceNote: 「粽橫四海」+「粽橫天下」諧音梗 from 2025 端午祝福語整理; rewritten as original colleague farewell scene

zh-tw.content:
公司端午節聚餐，剛好是阿傑離職前最後一天。

主管舉杯：「祝阿傑未來粽橫四海！」

大家：「粽橫四海！」

阿傑感動地舉杯。

主管：「也希望你下一份工作粽是順利。」

大家：「粽是順利！」

阿傑笑了。

主管：「最重要的是——粽於離開這個鬼地方了。」

大家沉默。

阿傑：「⋯⋯主管，這話我不敢接。」

主管：「沒關係，我下個月也走。」

全桌愣住三秒，然後爆笑。

端午節快樂，獻給所有「主管比員工還想離職」的台灣公司，記得粽子要冰起來，因為這頓飯之後氣氛已經回不去了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽橫四海」=「縱橫四海」、「粽是順利」=「總是順利」、「粽於」=「終於」——三個諧音梗連發，加上主管自爆要走的神轉折，是離職員工最爽的端午節，傳給那個剛遞辭呈的朋友
variations: 短版：「祝你粽橫四海，粽是順利，最重要的是——粽於可以擺脫前公司了。」

en.content:
Company Dragon Boat Festival dinner, also Jay's last day before quitting.

Manager raises a glass: "To Jay — may you zong-quer the world!"

Everyone: "Zong-quer the world!"

Jay raises his glass, touched.

Manager: "And may your next job zong-stantly succeed."

Everyone: "Zong-stantly succeed!"

Jay smiles.

Manager: "And most importantly — you've zong-finally escaped this hellhole."

The table goes quiet.

Jay: "...Boss, I can't respond to that one."

Manager: "It's fine. I'm leaving next month too."

Three seconds of frozen silence, then the whole table loses it.

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every company where the manager wants out more than the employees. Save the leftover zongzi — the vibes never recover from this dinner.
en.editorNote: Triple zongzi pun (zong sounds like 縱/總/終 in Chinese, meaning 'through,' 'always,' and 'finally') plus the boss confessing he's also quitting — peak farewell humor. Send to the friend who just handed in their notice
variations: Short version: "May your next chapter zong-quer the world, zong-stantly succeed, and zong-finally free you from this place."

items.sourceNote: 南部粽 vs 北部粽 永恆爭論 from Pixnet 端午節笑話集; rewritten as original family dinner argument

zh-tw.content:
端午節，全家圍著桌子吃粽子。

爸（南部人）：「這才叫粽子，水煮的，糯米Q彈。」

媽（北部人）：「拜託，那叫油飯加葉子吧？真正的粽子是用蒸的。」

爸：「蒸的那是飯糰，不是粽子。」

媽：「煮的那是肉粽湯，整顆軟爛沒口感。」

爺爺（中部人）：「你們都閉嘴。中部粽才是正統，半煮半蒸。」

爸媽同時：「⋯⋯爺爺，沒有人在吃中部粽。」

爺爺：「那是因為你們沒文化。」

阿姨（客家人）：「我們客家粄粽呢？」

全家沉默。

阿姨：「⋯⋯算了，我吃我的。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「家裡每年都因為粽子吵架」的台灣家庭。提醒：吵歸吵，最後還是會把對方那顆吃掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 南部粽（水煮）vs 北部粽（蒸）是台灣每年端午節必吵的話題，再加上中部粽和客家粄粽兩個邊緣選手——這是台灣餐桌宇宙真理，傳到家族群組準備引戰，效果保證
variations: 短版：「端午節吵粽子是台灣家庭傳統，吵完還是會吃對方那顆，這才是真正的團圓。」

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival. The whole family at the dinner table.

Dad (from the south): "This is a real zongzi — boiled, rice with proper bite."

Mom (from the north): "Please. That's just oily rice with a leaf. Real zongzi is steamed."

Dad: "Steamed is just a rice ball. That's not a zongzi."

Mom: "Boiled is just dumpling soup. Mushy, no texture."

Grandpa (from central Taiwan): "Both of you, quiet. Central-style zongzi is the original — half-boiled, half-steamed."

Mom and Dad together: "...Grandpa, nobody eats central zongzi."

Grandpa: "That's because you have no culture."

Aunt (Hakka): "What about our Hakka rice cake zongzi?"

The whole family goes silent.

Aunt: "...Never mind. I'll eat mine in peace."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every Taiwanese family that fights over zongzi style every single year. Reminder: you'll argue, then quietly eat the other side's one anyway.
en.editorNote: Southern-style (boiled) vs Northern-style (steamed) zongzi is the eternal Taiwanese family debate, with central-style and Hakka rice-cake zongzi as the underdog wildcards. Universal Taiwanese dinner table chaos — drop in the family group chat to start World War Zongzi
variations: Short version: "Arguing over zongzi style is a Taiwanese family tradition. The argument ends when both sides quietly eat the other's. That's real reunion."

items.sourceNote: 屈原跳江 + 現代抱怨 contrast humor from Dragon Boat Festival jokes compilation; rewritten as original riverside monologue

zh-tw.content:
端午節午夜，江邊。

屈原從江裡浮出來，看著手機螢幕的光。

屈原：「兩千三百年了，你們還在丟粽子給我？」

路人甲：「對啊，每年都丟。」

屈原：「拜託，我已經吃膩了。可以換點別的嗎？」

路人甲：「那你想吃什麼？」

屈原：「⋯⋯炸雞、珍奶、鹹酥雞。」

路人甲：「先生，你跳江是因為憂國憂民欸。」

屈原：「那是兩千多年前的事了，現在我只想吃宵夜。」

路人甲：「⋯⋯」

屈原：「順便問一下，你們那邊房價怎麼樣？」

路人甲：「⋯⋯先生，您還是繼續當水裡的傳奇好了。」

屈原默默沉回水裡。

端午節快樂，獻給所有「屈原如果穿越到 2026 年，可能會再跳一次」的現代生活見證者。
zh-tw.editorNote: 屈原從歷史人物變成 2026 年抱怨房價、想吃鹹酥雞的普通人——這個荒謬感是端午節最爽的黑色幽默。傳給那個房貸壓力很大、又愛吃宵夜的朋友，他會懂
variations: 短版：「屈原：兩千年了能不能換口味？路人：那您想吃什麼？屈原：炸雞珍奶。⋯⋯先生您還是回水裡吧。」

en.content:
Midnight on Dragon Boat Festival. By the river.

Qu Yuan rises from the water, blinking at a passerby's phone screen.

Qu Yuan: "Twenty-three hundred years and you're still throwing zongzi at me?"

Passerby: "Yeah, every year."

Qu Yuan: "Please. I'm sick of them. Can we switch it up?"

Passerby: "What would you like?"

Qu Yuan: "...Fried chicken. Bubble tea. Salty crispy chicken."

Passerby: "Sir, you jumped into a river out of patriotic despair."

Qu Yuan: "That was two thousand years ago. Right now I just want a midnight snack."

Passerby: "..."

Qu Yuan: "By the way — how's the housing market up there?"

Passerby: "...Sir, maybe just stay a legend in the water."

Qu Yuan silently sinks back down.

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to everyone who suspects that if Qu Yuan time-traveled to 2026, he'd jump in again — but this time over rent.
en.editorNote: Qu Yuan, the legendary patriot poet, downgraded into a modern guy complaining about rent and craving fried chicken — the absurdity is peak holiday dark humor. Send to the friend with a brutal mortgage and a late-night snack habit
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan: 'Can we switch from zongzi after 2,000 years?' Passerby: 'To what?' Qu Yuan: 'Fried chicken and bubble tea.' Passerby: 'Sir, please go back in the water.'"

items.sourceNote: 外籍員工誤會粽葉為「生菜」的辦公室趣事，源自端午節笑話集；改寫為原創新人入職對話

zh-tw.content:
公司端午節發粽子，新來的外籍同事 Mike 隔天進辦公室。

Mike：「謝謝你們的禮物，但我有個小問題。」

我：「怎麼了？」

Mike：「外面那層生菜煮太久，咬不動。」

我：「⋯⋯Mike，那是粽葉。」

Mike：「粽葉？」

我：「不能吃的，是用來包的。」

Mike 沉默了三秒。

Mike：「那為什麼有人吃完整顆都沒事？」

我：「⋯⋯誰？」

Mike：「我們經理，他昨天在茶水間整顆吞下去。」

我：「⋯⋯」

端午節快樂，提醒所有公司行政：發粽子前請附上使用說明書，特別是有外籍員工或我們經理那種人的辦公室。
zh-tw.editorNote: 外籍同事誤把粽葉當生菜+經理整顆吞下去的雙重笑點，是辦公室端午節經典場景，傳到公司群組保證有人對號入座，特別是那個總是不看說明書的同事
variations: 短版：「Mike：粽子外面那層生菜煮太久咬不動。我：那是粽葉。Mike：可是經理整顆吞下去欸。」

en.content:
The company handed out zongzi for Dragon Boat Festival. The next morning, our new foreign hire Mike walks in.

Mike: "Thanks for the gift, but quick question."

Me: "What's up?"

Mike: "The lettuce on the outside was overcooked. I couldn't bite through it."

Me: "...Mike, that's a bamboo leaf."

Mike: "Bamboo leaf?"

Me: "You don't eat it. It's just the wrapper."

Mike goes quiet for three seconds.

Mike: "Then why did someone eat the whole thing?"

Me: "...Who?"

Mike: "Our manager. He swallowed the whole one in the break room yesterday."

Me: "..."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival. Reminder to every office admin: include an instruction manual with the zongzi, especially if you have foreign hires or that one manager who never reads the label.
en.editorNote: Double punchline: foreign coworker mistakes bamboo leaves for lettuce, plus the manager who eats the whole thing. Classic office Dragon Boat Festival energy — drop in the company chat and watch someone get called out
variations: Short version: "Mike: 'The outer lettuce was too tough.' Me: 'That's a bamboo leaf.' Mike: 'But the manager swallowed the whole thing.'"

items.sourceNote: 屈原放假三天 vs 其他詩人留作業 學生視角梗，源自端午節笑話流行格式；改寫為原創課堂對話

zh-tw.content:
國文課，老師問：「同學們，你們最喜歡哪一位古代詩人？」

小明立刻舉手：「屈原。」

老師很欣慰：「為什麼？」

小明：「因為他最有良心。」

老師：「⋯⋯怎麼說？」

小明：「李白寫了那麼多詩，要背；杜甫寫了那麼多詩，也要背；蘇軾寫了那麼多詩，還是要背。」

老師：「然後呢？」

小明：「只有屈原，他死了之後留給我們三天連假。」

全班鼓掌。

老師：「⋯⋯下次月考你給我準備好。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「對屈原懷抱真誠感激」的學生與打工仔。提醒：放假是他給的，但加班是老闆給的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 學生最誠實的告白：屈原偉大不在於詩，在於三天連假。這是台灣每個經歷過國文課的人都懂的笑點，傳到大學群組或社畜群組都有效，特別是連假前一週
variations: 短版：「老師：你最喜歡哪個詩人？學生：屈原。老師：為什麼？學生：別人留詩給我們背，他留三天假給我們放。」

en.content:
Chinese literature class. The teacher asks, "Class, who's your favorite ancient poet?"

Ming's hand shoots up: "Qu Yuan."

Teacher, pleased: "Why?"

Ming: "Because he has the most conscience."

Teacher: "...Go on."

Ming: "Li Bai wrote a ton of poems — we have to memorize them. Du Fu wrote a ton — memorize. Su Shi wrote a ton — also memorize."

Teacher: "And?"

Ming: "Only Qu Yuan, after he died, left us a three-day weekend."

The whole class applauds.

Teacher: "...You'd better ace the next exam."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every student and 9-to-5 worker who genuinely thanks Qu Yuan. Reminder: he gave you the holiday, but your boss gave you the overtime.
en.editorNote: The most honest student take: Qu Yuan's real legacy isn't poetry — it's the three-day weekend. Universal experience for anyone who survived a literature class. Send to the group chat the week before the long weekend
variations: Short version: "Teacher: Favorite poet? Student: Qu Yuan. Teacher: Why? Student: Other poets left poems to memorize. He left us a long weekend."

items.sourceNote: Dragon boat 划船同事不出力 office team-building humor; 改寫為原創公司龍舟賽對話

zh-tw.content:
公司報名端午節龍舟比賽，划到一半。

隊長：「左邊！用力！」

大家：「呼！」

隊長：「右邊！跟上！」

大家：「呼！」

隊長：「中間那個誰，你的槳放下去了沒？」

小陳：「⋯⋯隊長，我的槳卡住了。」

隊長：「卡哪裡？」

小陳：「卡在我手機螢幕上。」

隊長：「⋯⋯你在划船划到一半滑手機？」

小陳：「我在傳訊息給我老婆說我快回家了。」

隊長：「我們才剛划五十公尺。」

小陳：「她不知道啊。」

結果他們最後一名。

端午節快樂，獻給所有「公司強迫報名團建活動」的社畜。提醒：划龍舟跟做專案一樣，總有一個人在划水，而那個人通常是中間那位。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「划水」一語雙關（划船 vs 摸魚不出力），加上小陳划船划到一半傳訊息給老婆的荒謬感——這是公司團建最真實的寫照。傳到公司群組準備引發共鳴，特別是那個被點名的同事
variations: 短版：「隊長：中間那個怎麼沒在划？小陳：我在傳訊息跟我老婆說快到家了。隊長：我們才划五十公尺。」

en.content:
The company signed up for the Dragon Boat Festival race. Halfway through:

Captain: "Left side, push harder!"

Team: "Hup!"

Captain: "Right side, keep up!"

Team: "Hup!"

Captain: "Middle seat — is your paddle even in the water?"

Chen: "...Captain, my paddle is stuck."

Captain: "Stuck where?"

Chen: "Stuck on my phone screen."

Captain: "...You're on your phone in the middle of a race?"

Chen: "I'm texting my wife I'll be home soon."

Captain: "We've gone fifty meters."

Chen: "She doesn't know that."

They finished dead last.

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every employee force-volunteered into corporate team-building. Reminder: dragon boating is like every group project — there's always one person coasting, and it's usually the one in the middle seat.
en.editorNote: The Chinese term 'paddle-splash' is also slang for slacking off — perfect double meaning, plus Chen texting his wife mid-race for full corporate-bonding chaos. Send to the office chat the morning of the company race
variations: Short version: "Captain: Why isn't the middle paddling? Chen: I'm texting my wife I'm almost home. Captain: We're fifty meters in."

items.sourceNote: 媽媽塞粽子+冰箱爆炸 端午節家庭場景，源自端午節祝福語與生活笑話集；改寫為原創母女通話

zh-tw.content:
端午節後一週，女兒打電話回家。

女兒：「媽，妳上次寄來的粽子我還沒吃完。」

媽：「沒關係，冰起來慢慢吃。」

女兒：「冰箱已經塞不下了。」

媽：「那妳分一點給朋友。」

女兒：「我已經分了。」

媽：「分多少？」

女兒：「全公司一人三顆。」

媽：「那剩多少？」

女兒：「⋯⋯三十顆。」

媽：「那再寄二十顆給妳。」

女兒：「媽，妳沒有在聽我說話。」

媽：「我有，妳冰箱塞不下，所以我下週寄個小冰箱給妳。」

女兒：「⋯⋯」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「媽媽寄粽子像在打仗」的台灣兒女。提醒：媽媽的愛是論斤秤的，而且永遠不會少於十顆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽寄粽子寄到冰箱爆炸還要再寄冰箱——這是台灣媽媽的經典操作，每個離家在外的兒女都懂。傳到媽媽群組或姐妹群組，保證引發大量「+1」回應
variations: 短版：「女兒：媽，冰箱塞不下粽子了。媽：那我再寄一個冰箱給妳。」

en.content:
A week after Dragon Boat Festival. Daughter calls home.

Daughter: "Mom, I haven't finished the zongzi you sent."

Mom: "No problem, just freeze them."

Daughter: "The freezer's full."

Mom: "Then share with friends."

Daughter: "Already did."

Mom: "How many?"

Daughter: "Three each, for the entire company."

Mom: "How many left?"

Daughter: "...Thirty."

Mom: "I'll send you another twenty."

Daughter: "Mom, you're not listening."

Mom: "I am. Your freezer's full, so next week I'm sending a small freezer too."

Daughter: "..."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every kid whose mom treats zongzi delivery like a military campaign. Reminder: a mom's love is measured in kilograms, and never less than ten dumplings.
en.editorNote: Mom sends zongzi until the freezer explodes, then sends a freezer too — peak Asian mom logic. Universal experience for anyone living away from home. Send to the family chat or the moms group for instant 'same' replies
variations: Short version: "Daughter: Mom, my freezer's full of zongzi. Mom: Then I'll send you a new freezer too."

items.sourceNote: Mother's Day Wi-Fi 比喻一句話笑話格式；改寫為原創母女對話與情境

zh-tw.content:
我跟我媽說：「妳知道妳跟家裡的Wi-Fi有什麼共同點嗎？」

媽：「什麼？」

我：「永遠在線、從來沒被感謝、出事第一個被怪。」

媽沉默了三秒。

媽：「那你下個月電費自己繳。」

我：「⋯⋯媽，母親節快樂。」

媽：「現在才想起來？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節經典自嘲——把媽媽比喻成Wi-Fi，永遠在線又永遠被當理所當然，結果被反將一軍。傳到家族群組或母親節限動，配個康乃馨emoji效果最好
variations: 短版：「媽媽就像家裡的Wi-Fi——永遠在線、從來沒被感謝、出事第一個被怪。」
情境版：母親節傳給媽：「妳是我心中最強的Wi-Fi。」媽回：「那你也記得繳網路費。」

en.content:
I told my mom: "You know what you have in common with our home Wi-Fi?"

Mom: "What?"

Me: "Always online, never thanked, first to get blamed when something breaks."

Mom went quiet for three seconds.

Mom: "Then you can pay the electricity bill next month."

Me: "...Mom, Happy Mother's Day."

Mom: "Just remembered now?"
en.editorNote: Mother's Day classic — moms as the household Wi-Fi: always on, never thanked, first to get blamed. Then she counters with a rent-bill threat. Pin it to the family chat or Mother's Day story with a carnation emoji
variations: Short version: "Mom is the original Wi-Fi: always connected, rarely thanked, first thing you blame when something goes wrong."
Card version: "Happy Mother's Day to my favorite Wi-Fi signal — I notice you most when you're gone."

items.sourceNote: 母親節送禮 媽媽嫌貴經典場景；改寫為原創母女通話與三段反轉

zh-tw.content:
母親節前一天，女兒打電話問媽。

女兒：「媽，明天母親節想要什麼？」

媽：「不用啦，妳健康快樂就好。」

女兒：「那我訂大餐。」

媽：「外面太貴。」

女兒：「那我送花。」

媽：「花會謝。」

女兒：「那我送包。」

媽：「我包包夠多了。」

女兒：「那我送現金。」

媽：「妳賺錢辛苦。」

女兒：「⋯⋯那妳到底要什麼？」

媽：「我說了不用啊。」

女兒：「那我什麼都不送。」

媽：「妳這樣對你媽？」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「問了等於沒問」的台灣媽媽。提醒：媽媽說不用，是測試題，不是放棄題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽說「不用」其實是反向題，這是台灣母親節的經典劇本——女兒每個提議都被打槍，最後乾脆放棄，又被罵不孝。傳到姐妹群組保證引發大量「我媽也是」的共鳴
variations: 短版：「媽：不用送禮。我：那不送了。媽：妳這樣對你媽？」
現金版：「媽：花太貴。我：那給現金。媽：妳賺錢辛苦。我：那妳到底要什麼？媽：我說不用就不用。」

en.content:
Day before Mother's Day. Daughter calls her mom.

Daughter: "Mom, what do you want tomorrow?"

Mom: "Nothing. As long as you're healthy and happy."

Daughter: "I'll book a fancy dinner."

Mom: "Too expensive."

Daughter: "Flowers, then."

Mom: "They'll just wilt."

Daughter: "A handbag?"

Mom: "I have enough bags."

Daughter: "Cash?"

Mom: "You work hard for that."

Daughter: "...So what do you actually want?"

Mom: "I told you, nothing."

Daughter: "Fine, I'll get nothing."

Mom: "That's how you treat your mother?"

Happy Mother's Day to every kid stuck in the 'I don't want anything' trap. Reminder: when mom says 'nothing,' it's a test, not a passing grade.
en.editorNote: The 'I don't want anything' trap — a universal mom move. Every option gets shot down, then giving up is also wrong. Send to the sibling group chat the night before Mother's Day for instant 'this is literally my mom' replies
variations: Short version: "Mom: Don't get me anything. Me: Okay, I won't. Mom: That's how you treat your mother?"
Cash version: "Daughter: Cash? Mom: You earned that. Daughter: So what do you want? Mom: Nothing means nothing."

items.sourceNote: 媽媽從三個房間外讓你愧疚 經典美式 mom jokes 格式；改寫為原創家庭對話

zh-tw.content:
兒子在房間打電動。

媽（從廚房）：「我不生氣。」

兒子瞬間冷汗。

媽：「你想打多久就打多久。」

兒子手在發抖。

媽：「反正我一個人洗碗也沒關係。」

兒子按下暫停。

媽：「我只是有點累而已。」

兒子站起來。

媽：「不用啦，我自己來。」

兒子已經到廚房了。

媽：「你看你，這麼緊張。」

兒子：「⋯⋯我什麼都還沒做。」

媽：「但你已經知道了。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有擁有「跨房間心電感應罪惡感發射器」的媽媽。提醒：媽媽不用罵你，她只要說「沒關係」就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽不用罵人，光說「沒關係」「我不生氣」就能讓你愧疚到自己跑去洗碗——這是亞洲媽媽的超能力。傳到男友群組或兄弟群組，立刻引發共鳴
variations: 短版：「媽：我不生氣。兒子瞬間站起來去洗碗。」
對話版：「媽：沒關係，我自己來就好。兒子：媽我馬上去！媽：看你這麼緊張幹嘛。」

en.content:
Son's gaming in his room.

Mom (from the kitchen): "I'm not mad."

Son breaks into a cold sweat.

Mom: "Play as long as you want."

His hands are shaking.

Mom: "It's fine, I'll do the dishes alone."

He pauses the game.

Mom: "I'm just a little tired."

He stands up.

Mom: "No need, I've got it."

He's already in the kitchen.

Mom: "Look how nervous you are."

Son: "...I haven't done anything yet."

Mom: "But you already knew."

Happy Mother's Day to every mom with a cross-room guilt-emission device. Reminder: she doesn't need to yell — "it's fine" gets the dishes done faster.
en.editorNote: Mom doesn't need to scold — just 'I'm not mad' and 'it's fine' and you're already in the kitchen washing dishes. Universal Asian-mom telepathy. Drop in the boys' group chat for instant 'my mom too' replies
variations: Short version: "Mom: I'm not mad. Son: *immediately starts doing dishes.*"
Telepathy version: "Mom can say 'I'm fine' from three rooms away and still make me feel guilty for breathing."

items.sourceNote: 母親節電腦 motherboard 諧音梗 + Google搜尋媽媽經典場景；改寫為原創家庭對話

zh-tw.content:
兒子問媽：「媽，妳知道為什麼電腦這麼聰明嗎？」

媽：「為什麼？」

兒子：「因為它有主機板（motherboard）——就是聽媽媽的話。」

媽：「那你電腦這麼聰明，你怎麼還是這麼笨？」

兒子：「⋯⋯因為我是無線連線，訊號不穩。」

媽：「那不是訊號問題，是你根本沒在聽。」

兒子：「媽，這個梗在母親節應該要笑一下吧。」

媽：「我笑了啊，在心裡。」

兒子：「那我看不到。」

媽：「你Google一下『媽媽的笑容』就有了。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「看不到媽媽笑容」的兒女。提醒：媽媽的笑通常藏在飯桌上、紅包裡、還有妳沒注意到的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「主機板=聽媽媽的話」是國外經典mom joke的中文化版，加上「Google一下媽媽的笑容」反諷數位世代——適合傳給工程師朋友的媽媽群組，理科宅笑點剛好
variations: 短版：「為什麼電腦這麼聰明？因為它有主機板，會聽媽媽的話。」
工程師版：「兒子：媽，我是無線連線，訊號不穩。媽：那不是訊號，是你沒在聽。」

en.content:
Son asks his mom: "Mom, why are computers so smart?"

Mom: "Why?"

Son: "Because they have a motherboard — they listen to mom."

Mom: "Then if computers are so smart, why are you still this dumb?"

Son: "...Because I'm on wireless. Bad signal."

Mom: "That's not signal. You're just not listening."

Son: "Mom, it's Mother's Day, you're supposed to laugh."

Mom: "I am. On the inside."

Son: "I can't see that."

Mom: "Google 'mom's smile' and you'll find it."

Happy Mother's Day to everyone who never catches their mom smiling. Reminder: it's hidden at the dinner table, in red envelopes, and in every moment you weren't paying attention.
en.editorNote: The classic 'motherboard = listens to mom' pun, plus 'Google mom's smile' for a digital-age twist. Tech-friendly humor — perfect for engineer kids' Mother's Day cards
variations: Short version: "Why are computers smart? Because they have a motherboard — they listen to mom."
Engineer version: "Son: I'm on wireless, bad signal. Mom: That's not signal, that's you not listening."

items.sourceNote: 「It's spicy = 我不想分」經典美式 mom joke 改寫為原創亞洲家庭餐桌場景

zh-tw.content:
母親節聚餐，餐桌上擺滿了菜。

小孩看到一盤新的炒花枝，眼睛發亮：「這個我可以吃嗎？」

媽媽：「這個很辣喔。」

小孩：「沒關係我吃辣。」

媽媽：「真的很辣。」

小孩：「我超能吃辣的妳又不是不知道。」

媽媽：「⋯⋯這個是大人的口味啦。」

小孩夾了一口，咬下去。

甜的。

小孩：「⋯⋯媽，這根本不辣。」

媽媽：「對啊。」

小孩：「那妳剛剛——」

媽媽：「『辣』在媽媽的字典裡，翻譯起來是『我不想跟你分』。」

小孩：「⋯⋯」

媽媽夾走最後三塊：「母親節欸，讓我一下。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有發明「這個很辣」「這個對小孩太刺激」「這個你不喜歡吧」三大護菜結界的台灣媽媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 亞洲媽媽的「辣」=「不想分」，這個翻譯密碼每個小孩成年後才懂。傳到家族群組或兄弟姐妹群組，會引發「我媽也是！」連環大爆料
variations: 短版：「媽媽說『這個很辣』翻譯起來其實是『我不想跟你分』。」
三大結界版：「『這個很辣』『這個對小孩太刺激』『這個你不喜歡吧』——媽媽的護菜三句經，小孩破關難度SSS。」

en.content:
Mother's Day dinner, table loaded with food.

Kid spots a new dish of stir-fried squid: "Can I have some?"

Mom: "It's spicy."

Kid: "That's fine, I eat spicy."

Mom: "Like, really spicy."

Kid: "You know I can handle it."

Mom: "...This one's more for adults."

Kid takes a bite.

It's sweet.

Kid: "...Mom, this isn't spicy at all."

Mom: "Correct."

Kid: "Then why did you—"

Mom: "In Mom dictionary, 'spicy' translates to 'I don't want to share.'"

Kid: "..."

Mom claims the last three pieces: "It's Mother's Day. Let me have this one."

Happy Mother's Day to every mom who invented the holy trinity of food-blocking spells: 'this is spicy,' 'this is too rich for kids,' and 'you wouldn't like it.'
en.editorNote: Universal mom code — 'it's spicy' actually means 'mine.' Drop in the family group chat for instant 'my mom too' avalanche. Works as a Mother's Day caption with a dinner photo
variations: Short version: "Mom says 'it's spicy.' Translation: 'I'm not sharing.'"
Trinity version: "Mom's three food-blocking spells: 'it's spicy,' 'it's too rich,' 'you wouldn't like it.' Boss-level final form: silently moves the plate."

items.sourceNote: 「toddler land-speed record + 嘴巴裡有什麼」經典 mom humor 改寫為原創媽媽自述

zh-tw.content:
母親節，閨蜜傳訊息給我：「今天你最大，想做什麼？」

我：「我想自己上一次廁所，門關起來，沒人在外面拍門。」

閨蜜：「⋯⋯這個願望很卑微。」

我：「我也想吃一頓熱的飯。從第一口到最後一口都是熱的。」

閨蜜：「然後呢？」

我：「我想看一集影集，從頭看到尾，中間沒有人問我『媽媽妳在幹嘛』。」

閨蜜：「再來？」

我：「我想知道我家小孩什麼時候從地上爬起來，會比『媽媽你看』還快。」

閨蜜：「最後一個？」

我：「我想知道為什麼我兒子可以在三秒內衝到客廳，但是我叫他刷牙他要走十分鐘。」

閨蜜：「這已經是物理問題了。」

我：「對，我問過科學家了。」

閨蜜：「他怎麼說？」

我：「他說：『因為刷牙不好玩。』然後他媽媽叫他洗碗，他用三秒消失在客廳。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「最大的願望就是上廁所不被打擾」的媽媽們。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽的母親節願望樸素到讓人心疼——上廁所、吃熱飯、看完一集——每個當媽的都笑著點頭。最後科學家自己也是兒子，這個反轉很台灣式自嘲
variations: 短版：「母親節最大的願望：自己上廁所，沒人拍門。」
媽媽聯盟版：「我們的共同願望：吃一頓從頭熱到尾的飯，看一集沒人打斷的影集，上一次門關得起來的廁所。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. My best friend texts: "You're the queen today. What do you want?"

Me: "I want to use the bathroom alone. With the door closed. No one banging on it."

Friend: "...That's a small dream."

Me: "I want to eat one hot meal. Hot from the first bite to the last."

Friend: "And?"

Me: "I want to watch one full episode of something without anyone asking 'Mom, what are you doing?'"

Friend: "More?"

Me: "I want to know why my kid can sprint to the living room in three seconds but takes ten minutes to walk to the bathroom for teeth-brushing."

Friend: "That's a physics problem."

Me: "I asked a scientist."

Friend: "What did he say?"

Me: "He said: 'Because brushing isn't fun.' Then his mom asked him to do the dishes and he vanished in three seconds."

Happy Mother's Day to every mom whose biggest wish is to pee alone.
en.editorNote: Mom's Mother's Day wishes are humble enough to break your heart — pee alone, eat something hot, finish one episode. Universal mom-life humor. Punchline: even the scientist is somebody's son. Great as a meme caption or sisterhood-of-moms text
variations: Short version: "Top Mother's Day wish: use the bathroom alone, with the door closed."
Mom alliance version: "Our shared dream: a meal that's hot start to finish, an episode no one interrupts, and a bathroom door that closes."

items.sourceNote: 「母親節在父親節之前 = 給孩子用聖誕節剩的錢」classic American holiday joke 改寫為原創兄妹對話

zh-tw.content:
兄妹群組，母親節前三天。

哥：「你準備買什麼給媽？」

妹：「還沒決定，預算抓兩千。」

哥：「兩千？！」

妹：「會太多嗎？」

哥：「會太少。」

妹：「我去年才送一千五。」

哥：「去年是去年，今年是今年。」

妹：「為什麼今年要漲？」

哥：「因為媽今年問了三次：『你哥送我什麼？』」

妹：「⋯⋯那意思是？」

哥：「意思是我有壓力，你也要有。」

妹：「但我預算就這樣⋯⋯」

哥：「你聖誕節不是有領年終？」

妹：「那筆錢我已經花掉了。」

哥：「花在哪？」

妹：「⋯⋯買禮物給男友。」

哥：「所以你男友比媽重要？」

妹：「不是這個意思——」

哥：「現在補救還來得及。」

妹默默點開購物網站，把預算改成三千。

母親節快樂，獻給所有「為什麼母親節在父親節前面，因為小孩要先把聖誕紅包花在媽身上」的家族經濟學。
zh-tw.editorNote: 兄妹群組為了媽媽禮物互相施壓的真實場景，最後一句揭曉「為什麼母親節在父親節前面」的家庭經濟學——傳到兄弟姐妹群組必爆笑，順便互相提醒今年該升級了
variations: 短版：「為什麼母親節在父親節前面？這樣小孩還來得及把聖誕節剩下的錢花在媽身上。」
兄妹施壓版：「哥：媽問了三次你送我什麼。妹：⋯⋯我馬上加預算。」

en.content:
Sibling group chat, three days before Mother's Day.

Brother: "What are you getting Mom?"

Sister: "Haven't decided. Budget around $80."

Brother: "$80?!"

Sister: "Too much?"

Brother: "Too little."

Sister: "Last year I spent $60."

Brother: "Last year was last year."

Sister: "Why the inflation?"

Brother: "Because Mom has asked me three times this week: 'What's your brother getting me?'"

Sister: "...Meaning?"

Brother: "Meaning I'm under pressure. So are you."

Sister: "But my budget—"

Brother: "Didn't you get a year-end bonus at Christmas?"

Sister: "...I already spent it."

Brother: "On what?"

Sister: "...A present for my boyfriend."

Brother: "So your boyfriend is more important than Mom?"

Sister: "That's not what I—"

Brother: "There's still time to fix this."

Sister silently opens her shopping app and bumps the budget to $120.

Happy Mother's Day. Reminder: Mother's Day comes before Father's Day so the kids still have leftover Christmas money to spend on Mom.

Dad gets the leftovers of the leftovers. Sorry, Dad.
en.editorNote: Sibling guilt-trip economics — older brother weaponizes 'Mom asked about you' to force budget inflation. Punchline reveals the family-economics theory of why Mother's Day comes first. Great for sibling group chats around early May
variations: Short version: "Mother's Day is before Father's Day so kids can spend leftover Christmas money on Mom. Dad gets the leftovers of the leftovers."
Pressure version: "Brother: Mom asked three times what you're getting her. Sister: ...adjusting budget now."

items.sourceNote: 「母親節送吸塵器 + 你說想要會吸的東西」經典美式 gift-gone-wrong 改寫為原創母子對話

zh-tw.content:
母親節，兒子送禮。

媽媽拆開包裝。

是一台吸塵器。

媽媽：「⋯⋯」

媽媽：「你送我吸塵器？」

兒子：「對啊，妳上禮拜不是說想要『會吸的東西』？」

媽媽：「⋯⋯」

媽媽：「我說的是『會把你的笑話吸走的東西』，因為你最近講話太爛了。」

兒子：「⋯⋯」

媽媽：「但這個吸塵器我收下了。」

兒子：「為什麼？」

媽媽：「因為它確實會吸——吸我的退休金。我看了一下價錢。」

兒子：「⋯⋯三千八而已。」

媽媽：「『而已』兩個字，是你工作以後才學會的，對吧。」

兒子：「⋯⋯」

媽媽（拿著吸塵器走進客廳）：「你上週掉在沙發底下的洋芋片屑，現在輪到它出馬了。」

兒子：「妳怎麼知道是我？」

媽媽：「因為你爸不吃洋芋片。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「禮物送錯但媽媽還是用得很開心」的兒女。
zh-tw.editorNote: 兒子送吸塵器引發媽媽神回擊——「會吸的東西=吸走你爛笑話」是雙關翻車經典，最後媽媽從沙發底下的洋芋片屑反推兇手是身為福爾摩斯的台灣媽媽日常
variations: 短版：「我說想要會吸的東西，是要吸走你爛笑話的，不是吸塵器。」
退休金版：「兒子：吸塵器三千八而已。媽：『而已』這兩個字是你工作後才學會的吧。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. Son hands Mom a gift.

She unwraps it.

It's a vacuum cleaner.

Mom: "..."

Mom: "You got me a vacuum cleaner?"

Son: "You said last week you wanted something that sucks."

Mom: "..."

Mom: "I meant something that sucks the bad jokes out of the room. Because lately, you."

Son: "..."

Mom: "But I'll keep the vacuum."

Son: "Why?"

Mom: "Because it does suck — it sucked $120 out of my retirement fund. I checked the price tag."

Son: "...That's not even that much."

Mom: "'Not even that much' — a phrase you learned after you started working, correct?"

Son: "..."

Mom (heading to the living room with the vacuum): "Those potato chip crumbs you dropped under the couch last week — your gift's first job."

Son: "How do you know it was me?"

Mom: "Your dad doesn't eat chips."

Happy Mother's Day to every kid whose gift missed the mark — but Mom uses it anyway, and somehow turns it into evidence against you.
en.editorNote: Vacuum-cleaner Mother's Day disaster with two punchlines: 'sucks out your bad jokes' wordplay and Mom's Sherlock-level couch-crumb forensics. Universal — works equally well in English. Great group-chat fodder around Mother's Day weekend
variations: Short version: "Mom: A vacuum? Son: You said you wanted something that sucks. Mom: I meant your jokes."
Detective version: "Mom: Those couch crumbs are yours. Son: How do you know? Mom: Your dad doesn't eat chips."

items.sourceNote: 「偉大的是媽媽、平凡的是我；交手機費的是媽媽、發簡訊的是我」對比結構梗，改寫為原創母親節前一晚對話

zh-tw.content:
母親節前一晚，我傳訊息給媽：

「媽，明天母親節快樂。」

「現在才幾點你就先說？」

「我怕明天起不來。」

「⋯⋯」

「媽，我跟妳說一句真心話。」

「你說。」

「偉大的是妳，懶惰的是我。」

「然後？」

「煮飯的是妳，吃飯的是我。」

「再來？」

「洗衣服的是妳，弄髒衣服的是我。」

「還有嗎？」

「繳手機費的是妳，傳這封訊息的也是我。」

媽過了五秒回：「所以你的意思是，這封訊息其實是我送自己的？」

「⋯⋯媽妳邏輯太強了。」

「我邏輯強，是因為養你養出來的。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「祝福語是媽出錢讓你發給她」的孩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典「偉大的是媽、平凡的是我」對比結構，最後神轉折是媽媽的吐槽——「這封訊息其實是我送自己的」，瞬間讓所有靠媽繳手機費的小孩坐立不安。傳到家族群組保證引發兄弟姐妹共同認領
variations: 短版：「偉大的是媽，懶惰的是我。煮飯的是媽，吃飯的是我。繳手機費的是媽，傳這則訊息的也是我。」
媽媽吐槽版：「所以這封母親節祝福，其實是我自己出錢買給自己的？」

en.content:
Night before Mother's Day. I text Mom:

"Mom, happy Mother's Day tomorrow."

"It's not even tomorrow yet."

"I'm scared I'll oversleep."

"..."

"Mom, real talk."

"Go on."

"You're the great one. I'm the lazy one."

"And?"

"You cook. I eat."

"Continue."

"You wash clothes. I dirty them."

"More?"

"You pay my phone bill. I send this text."

Five seconds pass. Mom replies:

"So basically, this Mother's Day message is one I bought for myself?"

"...Mom, your logic is too strong."

"My logic is strong because I raised you."

Happy Mother's Day to every kid whose 'thoughtful message' is technically funded by Mom.
en.editorNote: Contrast-structure joke flipped on its head: kid lists everything Mom does vs. what they do, then Mom delivers the killer line — 'this message was funded by me.' Universal hit for anyone still on a family phone plan
variations: Short version: "Mom does everything. I do nothing — except send this text, which she paid for."
Mom's logic version: "So this Mother's Day card is one I bought myself? — Mom, May 9th."

items.sourceNote: 「Mom's hug lasts long after she lets go because she won't let go」溫馨梗 + 「I deserve a medal for not selling you to the circus」改寫為原創客廳對話

zh-tw.content:
母親節，我張開雙手：「媽，給我一個擁抱。」

媽抱住我。

三秒後，我想放開。

媽沒放。

五秒。

媽還沒放。

十秒。

我：「媽⋯⋯」

媽：「再一下。」

二十秒。

我：「媽，我快不能呼吸了。」

媽：「再一下下。」

三十秒。

我：「媽，我等等還要去買蛋糕。」

媽：「⋯⋯你是不是只有今天才這樣抱我？」

我：「⋯⋯」

媽：「那再給我抱二十秒，補一下平常的份。」

我乖乖站好。

媽抱完，拍拍我的背：「你知道嗎，媽媽抱小孩的時間，總是比小孩想像的久。因為小孩會放手，媽媽不會。」

我鼻子一酸。

媽接著說：「而且你三歲的時候我抱你抱了五年沒喊累，你現在抱三十秒就喊累——我沒把你賣給馬戲團真的是我這輩子最大的克制。」

眼淚收回去了。

母親節快樂，獻給所有「擁抱十秒就想跑、被抓回來補抱二十秒」的孩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 前半溫馨催淚——「媽媽抱小孩，比小孩想像的久」，後半神轉折，媽用「我沒把你賣給馬戲團」破壞氣氛但又超真實，這種笑著流淚的節奏是台灣媽媽日常基本款
variations: 短版：「媽媽抱小孩的時間，總是比小孩想像的久——因為小孩會放手，媽媽不會。」
馬戲團版：「我沒把你賣給馬戲團，是我這輩子最大的克制。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. I open my arms: "Mom, hug?"

She hugs me.

Three seconds. I want to let go.

She doesn't.

Five seconds.

Still hugging.

Ten seconds.

Me: "Mom..."

Mom: "One more second."

Twenty seconds.

Me: "Mom, I can't breathe."

Mom: "Just a little longer."

Thirty seconds.

Me: "Mom, I have to go pick up the cake."

Mom: "...This is the only day a year you hug me like this, isn't it?"

Me: "..."

Mom: "Then give me twenty more seconds. To make up for the rest of the year."

I stand still.

She finally pulls back, pats my shoulder: "You know, a mom's hug always lasts longer than the kid expects. Because the kid lets go. The mom doesn't."

My nose stings.

Mom continues: "Also, when you were three, I held you for five years straight without complaining. You hugged me thirty seconds and started complaining. Not selling you to the circus was the greatest restraint of my life."

Tears: cancelled.

Happy Mother's Day to every kid who tries to escape the hug after ten seconds and gets dragged back for twenty more.
en.editorNote: Sweet-then-savage Mother's Day move: heartfelt 'a mom doesn't let go' line lands the tears, then Mom undercuts it with 'not selling you to the circus' — exactly how real moms balance love and roasting. Made for sentimental-but-ironic hearts
variations: Short version: "A mom's hug lasts longer than the kid expects. Because the kid lets go. The mom doesn't."
Circus version: "Not selling you to the circus was the greatest restraint of my life. — Mom"

items.sourceNote: 媽媽口頭禪「我不是說過了嗎」「電費是不用錢的嗎」家族群組梗改寫為原創母親節情境

zh-tw.content:
母親節，兄弟姐妹群組票選「媽媽年度金句」：

第五名：「電費是不用錢的嗎？」

第四名：「我不是說過了嗎？」

第三名：「你看誰誰誰家的小孩。」

第二名：「我等下要說的，你不要生氣喔。」（接下來一定會生氣）

第一名⋯⋯

大家正在猜。

弟：「是不是『冰箱有東西，自己看』？」

姐：「不對，是『我又沒怎樣』。」

哥：「都不對，是『隨便你』。」

媽突然在群組丟了一句：

「你們在票選什麼？」

大家瞬間靜音。

三秒後，妹打字：「⋯⋯母親節快樂。」

媽：「我又沒怎樣。」

「！！！」

「！！！！」

「！！！！！」

哥：「冠軍出爐了。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「以為媽不會看群組、結果媽其實一直在看」的兄弟姐妹。
zh-tw.editorNote: 兄弟姐妹偷偷票選媽媽口頭禪，結果媽本人空降——這個情境只要家族群組裡有媽媽就 100% 中招。「我又沒怎樣」是台灣媽媽宇宙級萬用回應，根本是金鐘獎終身成就獎得主
variations: 短版：「媽媽年度金句冠軍：『我又沒怎樣。』」
媽空降版：「兄妹群組偷偷選媽媽口頭禪，第一名揭曉前媽空降：『你們在票選什麼？』全員靜音。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. Sibling group chat votes for 'Mom's Catchphrase of the Year':

#5: "Is electricity free?"

#4: "Didn't I just tell you this?"

#3: "Look at so-and-so's kid."

#2: "Don't get mad at what I'm about to say." (You will get mad.)

#1...

Guesses pour in.

Brother: "'Food's in the fridge, find it yourself'?"

Sister: "No, it's 'I didn't do anything.'"

Older brother: "Wrong. It's 'Whatever you want.'"

Mom suddenly drops a message in the chat:

"What are you all voting on?"

Entire group goes silent.

Three seconds later, little sister: "...Happy Mother's Day."

Mom: "I didn't do anything."

"!!!"

"!!!!"

"!!!!!"

Older brother: "And we have a winner."

Happy Mother's Day to every sibling group that thought Mom wasn't reading the chat — and learned otherwise.
en.editorNote: Sibling group chat votes Mom's catchphrase of the year — Mom shows up mid-poll. 'I didn't do anything' wins by ambush. Universal: every culture has the mom phrase that means the opposite of what it says
variations: Short version: "Mom's catchphrase of the year, voted by her own kids: 'I didn't do anything.'"
Ambush version: "Sibling chat voting on Mom's catchphrases. Mom appears: 'What are you voting on?' Group goes silent."

items.sourceNote: 「Why is Mother's Day before Father's Day = so kids spend Christmas leftovers on Mom」+「mini-mum 短媽」+「motherboard 主機板」三組短梗合併改寫為母親節腦筋急轉彎家庭情境

zh-tw.content:
母親節，弟弟突然想表現一下，說：「媽，我問妳腦筋急轉彎。」

媽：「你問。」

弟：「為什麼電腦那麼聰明？」

媽：「為什麼？」

弟：「因為它有『主機板』，主機就是『母機』，所以它都聽媽媽的話。」

媽：「⋯⋯」

媽：「那你都不聽我的話，是因為你沒有主機板嗎？」

弟：「⋯⋯」

弟：「換一題。為什麼母親節在父親節前面？」

媽：「為什麼？」

弟：「因為這樣小孩才有時間把聖誕節剩下的錢花在媽身上。爸只能撿剩的。」

媽笑得很開心。

爸從房間走出來：「你們在說我什麼？」

媽：「沒有啊，弟在跟我說笑話。」

爸：「我也要聽。」

弟：「⋯⋯爸，再一個月就是父親節了。」

爸：「然後？」

弟：「我先告訴你，免得你到時候覺得驚喜不夠——預算可能會很少。」

爸：「⋯⋯為什麼？」

弟：「因為都花在媽身上了。」

爸轉頭看媽。

媽：「我又沒怎樣。」

母親節快樂，順便提前向爸道歉：剩下的錢真的不多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 三段式短笑話組合：主機板諧音梗（主機=母機）+ 母親節為何在父親節前的家庭經濟學 + 爸登場被預告父親節預算不足。最後媽再補一刀「我又沒怎樣」，是端到桌上的台式幽默套餐
variations: 腦筋急轉彎版：「為什麼電腦聰明？因為它有主機板，主機=母機，從小聽媽的話。」
對爸道歉版：「母親節快樂，順便提前跟爸說：父親節預算不多，因為都花在媽身上了。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. My little brother decides to show off:

"Mom, riddle time. Why are computers so smart?"

Mom: "Why?"

Brother: "Because they have a motherboard. They listen to their mother."

Mom: "..."

Mom: "Then why don't you listen to me? You don't have a motherboard?"

Brother: "..."

"Next one. Why is Mother's Day before Father's Day?"

Mom: "Why?"

Brother: "So the kids still have leftover Christmas money to spend on Mom. Dad gets the leftovers of the leftovers."

Mom laughs hard.

Dad walks out of his room: "What are you saying about me?"

Mom: "Nothing. Your son's telling jokes."

Dad: "I want to hear."

Brother: "...Dad, Father's Day is in about a month."

Dad: "And?"

Brother: "Just giving you a heads-up so it's not a surprise — your budget will be small."

Dad: "...Why?"

Brother: "Because we already spent it on Mom."

Dad turns to Mom.

Mom: "I didn't do anything."

Happy Mother's Day. And early apologies to Dad — there's really not much left.
en.editorNote: Three-pun combo: motherboard wordplay, the family-economics theory of Mother's-Day-first, and Dad walking in to learn his Father's Day budget got pre-spent. Mom's closing 'I didn't do anything' makes it a perfect Mother's Day eve send
variations: Riddle version: "Why are computers smart? They have a motherboard — they listen to their mother."
Sorry-Dad version: "Happy Mother's Day. P.S. Dad, Father's Day budget will be small. We already spent it on Mom."

items.sourceNote: 2026 馬年「馬→媽」諧音梗（做牛做馬→做牛做媽、鞍前馬後→鞍前媽後、老馬識途→老媽識途）改寫為母親節成語接龍家庭情境

zh-tw.content:
母親節吃飯，姐突然舉杯：「今天我們來玩『馬年成語送媽媽』。」

弟：「什麼意思？」

姐：「把成語裡的『馬』換成『媽』，看誰最會講。我先：『做牛做媽』——形容妳這三十年。」

媽眼眶一紅：「⋯⋯」

弟接：「『鞍前媽後』——小時候妳在前面開路，長大妳在後面墊背。」

媽眼眶更紅：「⋯⋯」

哥接：「『老媽識途』——我每次找不到東西，問妳一定知道在哪。」

媽：「⋯⋯」

媽：「你們真的很會。」

爸默默地舉杯：「我也要一個。」

全家：「來，爸。」

爸：「『一媽當先』——我們家所有事，妳永遠衝第一個。」

媽眼淚都要掉下來。

爸接著說：「然後我跟孩子們，是『馬後砲』。」

媽笑出來。

弟補一刀：「爸，你這個比喻⋯⋯妳到底是稱讚媽還是在貶低自己？」

爸：「都有，反正今天是媽的日子。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「做牛做媽、鞍前媽後」的台灣媽媽們。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 馬年限定母親節梗——「馬→媽」諧音四連發加上爸爸最後神補一刀「我們家是馬後砲」，前面催淚後面破壞氣氛但又自帶溫度。適合貼到家族群組或母親節當天聚餐照片下面
variations: 短版：「做牛做媽、鞍前媽後、老媽識途、一媽當先——獻給今天的妳。」
爸版：「我跟孩子是馬後砲，妳永遠是一媽當先。母親節快樂。」

en.content:
Mother's Day dinner. Sister raises her glass: "Tonight we play Year-of-the-Horse Idioms For Mom. Take any Chinese idiom with '馬' (horse), swap in '媽' (mom). Best one wins."

Brother: "Example?"

Sister: "'Working like a horse' becomes 'working like a mom.' That's been your last thirty years."

Mom's eyes get glassy.

Brother: "'Riding ahead and behind the horse' becomes 'ahead and behind Mom' — you cleared the road when we were small, you've got our back now we're grown."

Mom's eyes get redder.

Older brother: "'An old horse knows the way' becomes 'an old mom knows where everything is' — I've never lost anything you couldn't find."

Mom: "...You're all really laying it on."

Dad quietly raises his glass: "I want one too."

Family: "Go ahead, Dad."

Dad: "'Mom-first into battle' — anything happens in this family, you charge in first."

Mom is about to cry.

Dad: "And me and the kids? We're the cavalry that shows up after the fight's over."

Mom laughs.

Brother: "Dad — is that a compliment to Mom or a self-burn?"

Dad: "Both. It's her day."

Happy Mother's Day to every mom who's been working like a mom and charging in first.
en.editorNote: 2026 Year-of-the-Horse holiday combo — Mandarin pun where horse (马/mǎ) sounds like mom (妈/mā), so every horse idiom turns into a mom idiom. Dad's closing 'we're the cavalry that shows up after the fight' lands the laugh and the love at the same time
variations: Short version: "Working like a mom. Riding ahead and behind Mom. An old mom knows the way. Mom-first into battle. Today's all yours."
Dad version: "You're mom-first into battle. The rest of us are the cavalry that arrives late. Happy Mother's Day."

items.sourceNote: 「Thanks for not selling me to the circus」+「I forgot to put you in the will」黑色幽默梗合併改寫為母親節 LINE 對話原創情境

zh-tw.content:
母親節，我傳 LINE 給媽：「媽，母親節快樂，我忘記買禮物了。」

媽：「沒關係。」

我：「真的嗎？」

媽：「我也忘記把你寫進遺囑了。」

我：「？？？」

我：「媽，妳有遺囑？」

媽：「沒有，但你這樣我就要去寫一份。」

我：「⋯⋯我下午就去買禮物。」

媽：「不用啦，禮物我自己買，你只要負責付錢就好。」

我：「⋯⋯這跟我直接買有什麼不一樣？」

媽：「差很多。我自己買的我喜歡，你買的我可能會嫌。」

我：「媽，妳這個邏輯⋯⋯」

媽：「省下你被我嫌的時間，去做點有意義的事。」

我：「比如？」

媽：「比如想想為什麼三十年來只有你會忘記我生日跟母親節。」

我：「⋯⋯妳是不是其實很在意。」

媽：「我又沒怎樣。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「以為媽不在意、結果媽其實全部都記得」的孩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 黑色幽默母親節版——媽用「我也忘了把你寫進遺囑」反殺孩子的健忘，後面又補上「我又沒怎樣」收尾，嘴硬心軟的台灣媽媽精髓。適合在群組裡傳給那個老是忘記節日的兄弟姐妹
variations: 短版：「我忘記買母親節禮物。媽：沒關係，我也忘了把你寫進遺囑。」
邏輯版：「媽，禮物我買。媽：不用，我自己買你付錢就好——這樣我才不會嫌。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. I text Mom: "Happy Mother's Day. I forgot to get you a gift."

Mom: "No worries."

Me: "Really?"

Mom: "I also forgot to put you in the will."

Me: "???"

Me: "Mom, you have a will?"

Mom: "Not yet. But after that text I'm starting one."

Me: "...I'll go buy a gift this afternoon."

Mom: "Don't bother. I'll buy it. You just pay."

Me: "...How is that different from me just buying it?"

Mom: "Big difference. If I pick it, I like it. If you pick it, I find something wrong with it."

Me: "Mom, that logic—"

Mom: "Saves you the time of being criticized. Go do something meaningful instead."

Me: "Like what?"

Mom: "Like thinking about why, in thirty years, you're the only one of my kids who forgets my birthday AND Mother's Day."

Me: "...You actually care, don't you."

Mom: "I didn't do anything."

Happy Mother's Day to every kid who thinks Mom doesn't notice — and is wrong.
en.editorNote: Dark-humor Mother's Day. Mom counter-burns the forgotten gift with 'I also forgot to put you in the will,' lands the truth bomb 'you're the only kid who forgets,' then deflects with classic 'I didn't do anything.' Send to the sibling who's bad at holidays
variations: Short version: "Me: forgot your Mother's Day gift. Mom: No worries, I forgot to put you in the will."
Logic version: "Mom: I'll pick the gift, you pay. That way I actually like it. — A masterclass."

items.sourceNote: 「You're one in a melon」+「a-mom-zing」+「mini-mum」三組母親節英文諧音梗集合改寫為原創早餐情境

zh-tw.content:
母親節早上，我端著早餐進房間：「媽，起床。」

媽：「你怎麼這麼早？」

我：「因為妳是『媽』上的最棒。」

媽：「這是諧音梗嗎？」

我：「對，馬上→媽上。」

媽：「⋯⋯」

我：「來，吃西瓜。」

媽：「為什麼要吃西瓜？」

我：「因為妳是 one in a melon——萬中選一的媽。」

媽：「⋯⋯」

我：「妳真的 a-mom-zing。」

媽：「你今天是不是上網查了什麼？」

我：「沒有，是真心。」

媽：「真心會這樣連發三個諧音梗？」

我：「⋯⋯好啦，是看到你最棒。」

媽：「我想也是。」

媽吃了一口西瓜：「不過你還是『媽』上最棒的兒子。」

我：「真的嗎？」

媽：「真的。雖然你是我唯一的兒子。」

我：「⋯⋯所以那是基本款？」

媽：「是冠軍款，只是只有你一個參賽。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「諧音梗連發、被媽看穿但媽還是會接住」的孩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 中英諧音梗集中營——「馬上→媽上」「one in a melon」「a-mom-zing」三連發，加上媽最後神回「冠軍款只有你一個參賽」既溫柔又精準。適合早餐照片配文字、或母親節當天的限動
variations: 短版：「媽，妳是 one in a melon——萬中選一。」
兒子版：「我是冠軍兒子嗎？媽：是，只是參賽的只有你一個。」

en.content:
Mother's Day morning. I bring breakfast in: "Mom, wake up."

Mom: "You're up early."

Me: "Because you're a-mom-zing."

Mom: "Is that a pun?"

Me: "Yes. Mom + amazing."

Mom: "..."

Me: "Here, watermelon."

Mom: "Why watermelon?"

Me: "Because you're one in a melon."

Mom: "..."

Me: "You really are mom-derful."

Mom: "Did you Google these this morning?"

Me: "No, this is from the heart."

Mom: "From the heart? Three puns in a row?"

Me: "...Okay, I saw a list."

Mom: "Thought so."

Mom takes a bite of watermelon: "Still, you're my favorite son."

Me: "Really?"

Mom: "Really. Even though you're my only son."

Me: "...So that's the default?"

Mom: "It's the championship. There's just one contestant."

Happy Mother's Day to every kid who fires off three puns and gets caught — but gets the love anyway.
en.editorNote: Triple-pun breakfast combo — a-mom-zing, one in a melon, mom-derful — Mom catches the Google search but lands the warmest finish: 'championship with one contestant.' Pair with breakfast photo or Mother's Day morning IG story
variations: Short version: "Mom, you're one in a melon. Happy Mother's Day."
Champion version: "Am I your favorite son? Mom: Yes — only contestant in the championship."

items.sourceNote: 「Raising kids is a walk in the park — Jurassic Park」+「I need four business days to recover from each call」兩組梗合併改寫為原創母親節通話情境

zh-tw.content:
母親節，我打電話回家：「媽，母親節快樂！我以後每天都打電話給妳。」

媽：「不要。」

我：「⋯⋯為什麼？」

媽：「我每接一通你的電話，需要三個工作天才能恢復。」

我：「？？？」

媽：「你每次打來不是抱怨工作就是說錢不夠用，要不就是在質問我為什麼把你生這麼帥。」

我：「最後一個是讚美。」

媽：「在我這裡是控訴。」

我：「⋯⋯」

媽：「養小孩就像散步。」

我：「對啊，散步公園。」

媽：「侏羅紀公園。」

我：「⋯⋯」

媽：「我這一生最大的成就，就是把侏羅紀公園裡的迅猛龍養到會自己繳水電費。」

我：「⋯⋯媽，我是迅猛龍嗎？」

媽：「你是會撒嬌的迅猛龍。」

我：「⋯⋯這算讚美嗎？」

媽：「是事實。」

媽：「不過今天母親節，你打來我給你折扣——只要兩個工作天恢復就好。」

我：「⋯⋯謝謝媽。」

母親節快樂，獻給所有「以為自己長大了、其實在媽眼裡還是迅猛龍」的孩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽碎念進化版——「養小孩=侏羅紀公園、孩子=迅猛龍」霸氣比喻，加上「打電話需要三個工作天恢復」職場用語亂入家庭。最後媽給「母親節折扣兩個工作天」是隱藏溫柔。傳給最近被媽碎念過的朋友剛剛好
variations: 短版：「養小孩就像散步——侏羅紀公園。」
電話版：「媽說每接我電話需要三個工作天恢復——但今天母親節，折扣到兩個工作天。」

en.content:
Mother's Day. I call home: "Happy Mother's Day, Mom! I'm going to call you every day from now on."

Mom: "Please don't."

Me: "...Why?"

Mom: "I need three business days to recover from each call."

Me: "???"

Mom: "Every call you either complain about work, complain about money, or ask why I made you this handsome."

Me: "That last one's a compliment."

Mom: "Sounds like an accusation here."

Me: "..."

Mom: "Raising kids is a walk in the park."

Me: "Right, fresh air, sunshine—"

Mom: "Jurassic Park."

Me: "..."

Mom: "My greatest achievement in life is raising a velociraptor that pays his own utility bills."

Me: "...Mom, am I the velociraptor?"

Mom: "You're the velociraptor that whines for snacks."

Me: "...Is that a compliment?"

Mom: "It's a fact."

Mom: "But it's Mother's Day, so I'm giving you a discount — only two business days to recover from this call."

Me: "...Thanks, Mom."

Happy Mother's Day to every grown kid who's still a velociraptor in their mom's eyes.
en.editorNote: Mom-roast escalator: 'three business days to recover' (corporate speak invading family), 'raising kids is Jurassic Park,' velociraptor metaphor, then the soft landing — Mother's Day discount of two business days. Send to the friend whose mom roasts hardest
variations: Short version: "Raising kids is a walk in the park. Jurassic Park."
Phone version: "Mom: I need three business days to recover from each of your calls. Today is Mother's Day so it's only two."

items.sourceNote: 「Red, white, and brew」+「United we stand, divided we grill」兩組 Memorial Day BBQ 諧音梗合併改寫為原創夫妻烤肉對話

zh-tw.content:
Memorial Day 早上，老公在後院烤肉，我端著啤酒走出去：「老公，這就是傳說中的紅白藍嗎？」

老公：「什麼紅白藍？」

我：「紅肉、白啤酒泡、藍色心情——因為烤焦了。」

老公看了一眼烤架：「⋯⋯妳怎麼知道？」

我：「煙都飄到鄰居家了。」

老公：「那是愛國的煙。」

我：「⋯⋯」

老公：「Memorial Day 就是要 united we stand, divided we grill——團結站著、分開烤肉。」

我：「分開烤肉是什麼意思？」

老公：「就是你烤你的、我烤我的，不要管我。」

我：「⋯⋯你是不是不會烤？」

老公：「我是策略性失敗，這樣下次你就會自己烤。」

我：「⋯⋯這是你的紀念日策略？」

老公：「對，紀念我從此不用烤肉的偉大日子。」

我：「⋯⋯老公，這是 Memorial Day 不是你的解放紀念日。」

老公：「對我來說是一樣的。」

Memorial Day 快樂，獻給所有「烤肉烤到老婆親自上場」的老公們。
zh-tw.editorNote: Memorial Day 美式烤肉夫妻日常——「紅白藍」變「紅肉白啤酒藍心情」、「united we stand, divided we grill」直翻成「分開烤肉」的策略性失敗。適合美國節日當天傳給有烤肉技能落差的伴侶或朋友
variations: 短版：「Memorial Day 烤肉哲學——united we stand, divided we grill。」
策略版：「老公烤焦肉說是策略性失敗，這樣老婆下次就會自己烤。」

en.content:
Memorial Day morning. My husband's grilling in the backyard. I walk out with a beer: "Honey, is this the famous red, white, and blue?"

Husband: "What red, white, and blue?"

Me: "Red meat, white beer foam, blue mood — because you burned it."

Husband glances at the grill: "...How'd you know?"

Me: "The smoke is reaching the neighbors."

Husband: "That's patriotic smoke."

Me: "..."

Husband: "Memorial Day is about united we stand, divided we grill."

Me: "What does divided we grill mean?"

Husband: "You grill yours, I grill mine, leave me alone."

Me: "...You don't know how to grill, do you?"

Husband: "This is strategic failure. So next time, you grill."

Me: "...That's your holiday strategy?"

Husband: "Yes. Commemorating the day I never grill again."

Me: "...Honey, it's Memorial Day. Not your personal liberation day."

Husband: "Same thing to me."

Happy Memorial Day to every spouse who's been strategic-failured into taking over the grill.
en.editorNote: Memorial Day BBQ couple comedy — flips 'red, white, and blue' into red meat / white foam / blue mood (burned), and 'united we stand, divided we grill' into a husband's strategic-failure scheme to never grill again. Perfect Memorial Day text to any friend with a grill-skill gap at home
variations: Short version: "Memorial Day BBQ rule: united we stand, divided we grill."
Strategic version: "He burned the steaks on purpose. Calls it strategic failure so I'll grill next time."

items.sourceNote: 「What's a veteran's favorite dessert? Sweet victory」+「Grill of Duty」雙重梗改寫為原創退伍軍人爺孫對話情境

zh-tw.content:
Memorial Day，我問當過兵的爺爺：「爺爺，你最喜歡哪一種甜點？」

爺爺：「Sweet victory.」

我：「⋯⋯那不是甜點。」

爺爺：「在我那年代，能吃到糖就是勝利。」

我：「⋯⋯」

爺爺：「那妳知道我最愛玩的遊戲嗎？」

我：「決勝時刻？」

爺爺：「Grill of Duty——烤肉時刻。」

我：「⋯⋯爺爺，這是諧音梗。」

爺爺：「打過真槍的人，才有資格玩諧音梗。」

我：「⋯⋯」

爺爺把肉翻面：「孫女，妳知道為什麼老兵都會烤肉嗎？」

我：「因為退伍以後有時間？」

爺爺：「因為我們知道，火候要慢、肉要等、人要活著回來。」

我：「⋯⋯爺爺。」

爺爺：「來，吃一塊，這是 sweet victory。」

我：「⋯⋯這明明是肋眼。」

爺爺：「在我這裡，每一餐都是 sweet victory。」

Memorial Day 快樂，獻給所有「諧音梗背後其實藏著故事」的家人。
zh-tw.editorNote: Memorial Day 爺爺版——表面玩 sweet victory / Grill of Duty 兩個諧音梗，後面爺爺一句「火候要慢、肉要等、人要活著回來」突然把笑話收成深情。適合家裡有退伍長輩的人在節日傳給家族群組
variations: 短版：「爺爺最愛的甜點是 sweet victory，最愛的遊戲是 Grill of Duty。」
深情版：「老兵知道——火候要慢、肉要等、人要活著回來。」

en.content:
Memorial Day. I ask my grandpa, who served: "Gramps, what's your favorite dessert?"

Grandpa: "Sweet victory."

Me: "...That's not a dessert."

Grandpa: "In my day, sugar was a victory."

Me: "..."

Grandpa: "And do you know my favorite game?"

Me: "Call of Duty?"

Grandpa: "Grill of Duty."

Me: "...Gramps, that's a pun."

Grandpa: "You've gotta have fired a real gun to earn a pun like that."

Me: "..."

Grandpa flips the steak: "Kid, you know why all the old vets love grilling?"

Me: "Because you have time after retirement?"

Grandpa: "Because we learned this: low heat, slow patience, and everyone comes home alive."

Me: "...Gramps."

Grandpa: "Here. Try this. Sweet victory."

Me: "...It's just a ribeye."

Grandpa: "Every meal at this table is sweet victory."

Happy Memorial Day to every family whose dad jokes are hiding real stories.
en.editorNote: Memorial Day grandpa edition — opens with the puns (sweet victory, Grill of Duty), then lands the gut-punch line 'low heat, slow patience, and everyone comes home alive.' Send to anyone with a vet in the family on the holiday
variations: Short version: "Grandpa's favorite dessert: sweet victory. Favorite game: Grill of Duty."
Heartfelt version: "Old vets know — low heat, slow patience, everyone comes home alive."

items.sourceNote: 「粽是」「粽要」「粽歸」端午諧音祝福語格式改寫為原創公司群組搞笑情境

zh-tw.content:
公司群組，端午節前一天，老闆在群組裡發：「祝大家粽是有福、粽要平安、粽歸如意。」

同事 A：「老闆好有文采。」

同事 B：「老闆好有心。」

我：「老闆，粽歸如意是什麼意思？」

老闆：「就是不管怎樣，最後都會如意的意思。」

我：「⋯⋯所以這禮拜的提案如果被退，老闆會說粽歸如意嗎？」

老闆：「⋯⋯」

同事 A：「⋯⋯」

同事 B：「⋯⋯」

老闆：「補充：粽要堅強、粽要努力、粽要不要在群組發這種話。」

我：「⋯⋯」

同事 A 私訊我：「你完了。」

同事 B 私訊我：「老闆剛剛把粽子福袋從你那份拿走了。」

我：「⋯⋯」

老闆在群組裡：「最後一個粽——粽算了，端午節快樂。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「在群組裡口無遮攔、結果連粽子都沒得拿」的上班族。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節公司群組版——「粽是、粽要、粽歸」三組諧音祝福被員工拿來婊老闆，老闆神反擊「粽要不要在群組發這種話」，最後一句「粽算了」收尾超台。適合傳給辦公室裡那個總是亂講話的同事
variations: 短版：「粽是有福、粽要平安、粽歸如意——粽算了，老闆說的。」
員工版：「老闆說粽歸如意，所以提案被退也是如意？老闆：粽要不要在群組發這種話。」

en.content:
Company chat, day before Dragon Boat Festival. Boss posts: "Wishing everyone always-blessed, always-safe, always-in-luck this Dragon Boat Festival."

Coworker A: "Boss is so eloquent."

Coworker B: "Boss is so thoughtful."

Me: "Boss, what does always-in-luck mean exactly?"

Boss: "It means no matter what happens, things work out in the end."

Me: "...So if my proposal gets rejected this week, will Boss say it's always-in-luck?"

Boss: "..."

Coworker A: "..."

Coworker B: "..."

Boss: "Addendum: always-strong, always-hardworking, always-do-not-post-this-kind-of-message-in-the-group-chat."

Me: "..."

Coworker A DMs me: "You're done."

Coworker B DMs me: "Boss just removed the zongzi gift bag from your share."

Me: "..."

Boss in the group: "Final wish — always-forget-it. Happy Dragon Boat Festival."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every office worker who runs their mouth in the group chat and ends up with no zongzi.
en.editorNote: Dragon Boat Festival office group chat — three classic 'always-X' blessings (a Mandarin pun where 粽/zòng = always/总) get weaponized by an employee against the boss, who counter-blesses 'always-do-not-post-this-in-group-chat' and ends with 'always-forget-it.' Send to that coworker who can't stop pushing it in the company group
variations: Short version: "Boss's Dragon Boat blessing: always-blessed, always-safe, always-forget-it."
Employee version: "Boss: 'always-in-luck' means it all works out. Me: even rejected proposals? Boss: always-do-not-post-this-here."

items.sourceNote: 「手機用粽葉包好煮 60 分鐘延長電池」+「肉粽甜粽鹹粽不收只收現金」兩組端午梗合併改寫為原創媽媽 LINE 對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一週，媽傳 LINE：「兒子，今年端午回不回來？」

我：「媽，我加班，可能沒辦法。」

媽：「那我寄粽子給你。要甜的還是鹹的？」

我：「鹹的。」

媽：「南部還是北部？」

我：「南部。」

媽：「⋯⋯你住北部喔。」

我：「但我愛吃南部粽。」

媽：「兒子，你這樣會引戰。」

我：「在我們家還會嗎？」

媽：「會。我是北部人。」

我：「⋯⋯」

媽：「但沒關係，今年端午我家不收粽，只收現金。」

我：「？？？」

媽：「為了避免南北粽紛爭，省略實物環節，直接折現。」

我：「媽，這跟過年紅包有什麼不一樣？」

媽：「過年是父母給小孩，端午是小孩給父母。」

我：「⋯⋯」

媽：「對了，順便提醒你——手機用粽葉包好煮 60 分鐘可以延長電池續航。」

我：「媽，那是假的。」

媽：「我知道，但你要是真的用了，我就知道你忙到大腦也煮成粽子了。」

我：「⋯⋯」

媽：「快回來吃飯。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「以為媽是傳統媽媽、其實媽比你還會玩梗」的孩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節媽媽版——「南北粽戰爭」「不收粽只收現金」「手機包粽葉煮 60 分鐘」三個網路經典梗串成一通 LINE 對話，最後媽用「煮成粽子的大腦」反殺再用「快回來吃飯」收尾。適合在端午前一週傳給離家工作的朋友
variations: 短版：「媽：今年我家不收粽，只收現金，避免南北戰爭。」
假梗版：「媽：手機包粽葉煮 60 分鐘能延長續航。我：假的。媽：我知道，測你忙不忙。」

en.content:
Week before Dragon Boat Festival. Mom texts: "Coming home this year?"

Me: "Mom, I'm working overtime. Probably not."

Mom: "Then I'll mail zongzi. Sweet or savory?"

Me: "Savory."

Mom: "Southern style or northern style?"

Me: "Southern."

Mom: "...You live in the north."

Me: "But I prefer southern zongzi."

Mom: "Son, you're starting a war."

Me: "In our own family?"

Mom: "Yes. I am from the north."

Me: "..."

Mom: "Fine. This year my house doesn't accept zongzi. Only cash."

Me: "???"

Mom: "To avoid the north-south zongzi war, we are skipping the physical product and going straight to cash equivalent."

Me: "Mom, how is this different from Lunar New Year red envelopes?"

Mom: "Lunar New Year, parents give kids. Dragon Boat Festival, kids give parents."

Me: "..."

Mom: "Also, reminder — wrapping your phone in bamboo leaves and boiling it for 60 minutes extends battery life."

Me: "Mom, that's fake."

Mom: "I know. But if you actually tried it, I'd know your brain has been boiled into a zongzi too."

Me: "..."

Mom: "Come home. Eat."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every kid who thought their mom was traditional — and got out-memed.

(Translator's note: in Taiwan, southern-style zongzi are boiled, northern-style are steamed, and arguing which is better is a national pastime.)
en.editorNote: Dragon Boat Festival mom edition — three viral Taiwanese memes (north-south zongzi war, 'we only accept cash this year,' wrap-your-phone-in-bamboo-leaves) strung into one mom chat, ending with the savage 'your brain has been boiled into a zongzi too' and the soft landing 'come home, eat.' Send to the friend working through the holiday
variations: Short version: "Mom: This year we don't accept zongzi. Only cash. To avoid the north-south war."
Fake-hack version: "Mom: boil your phone in bamboo leaves for battery life. Me: that's fake. Mom: I know — I was testing how fried your brain is."

items.sourceNote: 「工作粽是被誇、生活粽是美夢」類型賀詞格式 from 2026 端午賀詞整理；改寫為原創 HR 與工程師 LINE 群組情境

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一天，HR 在群組發訊息：
「祝大家工作粽是順利、薪水粽是上漲、健康粽是滿分。」

工程師小林：「HR，那 bug 呢？」

HR：「⋯⋯」

小林：「bug 是不是也粽是出現？」

HR：「我們先不要破壞氣氛。」

主管：「小林，你今天加班。」

小林：「為什麼？」

主管：「因為你粽是講話。」

小林：「⋯⋯」

HR 補一句：「祝小林粽是被點名、粽是加班、粽是學會閉嘴。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「粽是在群組多嘴一句、然後粽是被加班」的工程師。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粧是」=「總是」端午諧音三連發，最後反殺工程師——適合傳給那個在公司群組總是要回一句的同事，或剛被點名加班的朋友。HR 跟主管聯手出招的畫面感是笑點
variations: 短版：「HR：祝大家粽是順利。工程師：那 bug 呢？HR：⋯⋯」
主管版：「主管：你加班。我：為什麼？主管：因為你粽是講話。」

en.content:
Day before Dragon Boat Festival. HR posts in the group chat:
"Wishing everyone always-smooth at work, always-rising salaries, always-perfect health."

Engineer Lin: "HR, what about bugs?"

HR: "..."

Lin: "Are bugs also always-present?"

HR: "Let's not ruin the mood."

Manager: "Lin, you're working overtime today."

Lin: "Why?"

Manager: "Because you're always-talking."

Lin: "..."

HR adds: "Wishing Lin always-called-out, always-overtime, always-learning-to-shut-up."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every engineer who said one extra thing in the group chat and ended up always-on-call.
en.editorNote: Three 'always-X' puns chained together (粽/zòng sounds like 總/always) — HR's well-wishes get hijacked by the engineer, then weaponized back. Send to the coworker who can't resist replying in the company group chat
variations: Short version: "HR: wishing everyone always-smooth. Engineer: what about bugs? HR: ..."
Manager version: "Manager: you're working overtime. Me: why? Manager: because you're always-talking."

items.sourceNote: 「想紀念 365 位歷史人物換假期」端午梗圖概念；改寫為原創公司會議室對話

zh-tw.content:
公司週會，主管：「下週端午連假三天，請大家妥善規劃工作。」

阿凱：「主管，我有個提議。」

主管：「請說。」

阿凱：「我們能不能紀念一下孔子？」

主管：「為什麼？」

阿凱：「孔子那麼偉大，總要放一天吧。」

主管：「⋯⋯」

阿凱：「還有孟子、李白、杜甫、白居易⋯⋯」

主管：「阿凱，你到底想說什麼？」

阿凱：「我查過了，一年三百六十五天，剛好可以紀念三百六十五個古人。」

主管：「所以呢？」

阿凱：「所以全年無休——的休息。」

主管沉默了五秒。

主管：「阿凱，那我們從紀念你開始。」

阿凱：「為什麼？」

主管：「因為你已經被資遣了，可以好好被紀念了。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「在會議上提了一個好點子、然後被點名紀念」的員工。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「365 位古人 = 365 天假期」是端午經典網路梗，改寫成會議室對話，主管以「紀念你」反殺收尾——傳給那個總是在會議上開玩笑、然後被主管盯上的同事最應景
variations: 短版：「阿凱：我們紀念 365 個古人，就能全年放假。主管：那從紀念你開始。」
員工版：「我：紀念屈原放一天。主管：紀念你放永遠。」

en.content:
Weekly meeting. Manager: "Three-day Dragon Boat weekend coming up. Plan your work accordingly."

Kai: "Boss, I have a suggestion."

Manager: "Go ahead."

Kai: "Can we commemorate Confucius?"

Manager: "Why?"

Kai: "He's a great man. We should give him at least one day off."

Manager: "..."

Kai: "Also Mencius, Li Bai, Du Fu, Bai Juyi..."

Manager: "Kai, what's your point?"

Kai: "I did the math. 365 days a year, 365 historical figures to commemorate."

Manager: "And?"

Kai: "And we'd get a full year of paid rest."

Five seconds of silence.

Manager: "Kai, let's start by commemorating you."

Kai: "Why?"

Manager: "Because you've been let go. Now you can rest in perpetual honor."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every employee who pitched a great idea in a meeting and got commemorated immediately.
en.editorNote: The viral '365 historical figures = 365 days off' joke reframed as a meeting scene. The manager's 'we'll start by commemorating you' is a classic Taiwanese office burn. Send to the coworker who never knows when to stop pitching ideas
variations: Short version: "Kai: let's commemorate 365 historical figures and get the whole year off. Manager: let's start with you."
Employee version: "Me: one day off for Qu Yuan. Manager: forever off for you."

items.sourceNote: 'Boats always leak' / 'drag-gone boat' English Dragon Boat puns; rewritten as original couple texting scene

zh-tw.content:
端午節早上，男友傳訊息：「寶貝，今天我要去划龍舟。」

女友：「好，注意安全。」

男友：「你不擔心嗎？」

女友：「擔心什麼？」

男友：「我們船長說，每艘龍舟都會漏水。」

女友：「那叫什麼船？」

男友：「叫做⋯⋯也許這就是為什麼英文叫 dragon boat——drag gone，拖下去就沒了。」

女友：「⋯⋯」

男友：「我這個冷笑話有沒有讓妳笑？」

女友：「沒有。但你如果沉了，我會幫你保險理賠笑出來。」

男友：「⋯⋯」

女友：「快去划，我等你的保險金。」

男友：「⋯⋯寶貝，這算情話嗎？」

女友：「算，獻給粽是想討我笑的你。」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「努力講冷笑話、但只成功讓另一半冷掉」的情侶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 中英混合諧音梗——「dragon boat」拆成「drag gone」（拖下去就沒了），女友的保險梗反殺成功——傳給喜歡冷笑話的另一半，或在端午聚會上講給朋友聽，重點是反殺要快，不要等對方笑
variations: 短版：「男友：dragon boat 就是 drag gone。女友：你沉了我笑得出來。」
情話版：「粽是想討妳笑的我，連龍舟都願意翻一艘。」

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival morning. Boyfriend texts: "Babe, I'm racing dragon boats today."

Girlfriend: "Okay, be safe."

Boyfriend: "You're not worried?"

Girlfriend: "About what?"

Boyfriend: "Our captain says every dragon boat leaks."

Girlfriend: "Why's that?"

Boyfriend: "Maybe that's why it's called a dragon boat — drag-gone boat. Drag it in, it's gone."

Girlfriend: "..."

Boyfriend: "Did my dad joke make you laugh?"

Girlfriend: "No. But if you sink, I'll laugh while filing the insurance claim."

Boyfriend: "..."

Girlfriend: "Now go paddle. Mama needs her payout."

Boyfriend: "...babe, was that a love note?"

Girlfriend: "Yes. For the man who always-tries to make me laugh."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every couple where one tells dad jokes and the other tells better ones back.
en.editorNote: Bilingual pun — 'dragon boat' broken as 'drag-gone' (drag it in, it's gone), then the girlfriend counters with the insurance payout joke. Send to the partner who can't stop with the dad jokes — the comeback is the real punchline
variations: Short version: "BF: dragon boat = drag-gone. GF: if you sink, I'll laugh during the claim."
Pickup version: "For the girl I'd flip a whole dragon boat to make laugh."

items.sourceNote: 「包中」=「包粽」考季諧音梗 + 端午阿嬤廢話文學格式；改寫為原創阿嬤孫子準備國考情境

zh-tw.content:
阿嬤包粽子，邊包邊唸：「給你吃這顆，包中喔。」

孫子：「阿嬤，我又沒考試。」

阿嬤：「那給你吃這顆，包甲。」

孫子：「什麼包甲？」

阿嬤：「包你身體甲意（強壯），這樣才能去考試。」

孫子：「我就說我沒考試。」

阿嬤：「那給你吃這顆。」

孫子：「這顆是什麼？」

阿嬤：「包你阿嬤心情好。」

孫子：「⋯⋯」

阿嬤：「不然你以為我為什麼從早上五點就開始包粽？」

孫子：「為了端午節？」

阿嬤：「為了包中我心情。」

孫子：「⋯⋯阿嬤，妳這個諧音梗。」

阿嬤：「你阿公也是這樣被我迷倒的。」

孫子：「⋯⋯」

端午節快樂，獻給所有「以為阿嬤只會包粽子、結果阿嬤連諧音梗都會玩」的孫子們。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「包中」=「包粽」考季經典諧音 + 台語「甲意」+ 阿嬤反殺三連發——適合傳到家族群組，尤其家裡有國考生或學測生時效果最好；阿公那句是隱藏笑點，會讓長輩們會心一笑
variations: 短版：「阿嬤：吃這顆包中。我：沒考試。阿嬤：那包我心情好。」
考生版：「阿嬤：吃這顆包甲。我：什麼甲？阿嬤：包你榜上有名甲第一。」

en.content:
Grandma wraps zongzi, muttering as she works: "Eat this one, guaranteed to pass."

Grandson: "Grandma, I don't have an exam."

Grandma: "Then eat this one. Guaranteed strong."

Grandson: "Strong how?"

Grandma: "Strong enough to take an exam."

Grandson: "I just said I don't have an exam."

Grandma: "Then eat this one."

Grandson: "What's this one for?"

Grandma: "Guaranteed to put Grandma in a good mood."

Grandson: "..."

Grandma: "Why else do you think I've been wrapping since five a.m.?"

Grandson: "For Dragon Boat Festival?"

Grandma: "For my mood."

Grandson: "...Grandma, that's a pun."

Grandma: "This is how I won your grandpa over."

Grandson: "..."

Happy Dragon Boat Festival to every grandchild who thought their grandma only knew how to wrap zongzi — and got out-punned at the kitchen table.
en.editorNote: Classic Taiwanese exam-season pun where 包粽/bāo zòng (wrapping zongzi) sounds like 包中/bāo zhòng (guaranteed pass). Grandma's escalating wishes and the throwaway 'this is how I won your grandpa over' is the warm landing. Send to family group chats during exam season
variations: Short version: "Grandma: eat this, guaranteed pass. Me: no exam. Grandma: then guaranteed my good mood."
Exam-taker version: "Grandma: eat this for top marks. Me: what marks? Grandma: any marks, as long as you pass."

items.sourceNote: 「欲擒故縱」=「玉琴顧粽」田馥甄式名字諧音梗格式；改寫為原創辦公室加班情境

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一天，老闆走進辦公室問：「明天誰要加班？」

大家沉默。

老闆：「那我點名。小陳？」

小陳：「我家有粽子要吃。」

老闆：「小李？」

小李：「我阿嬤包了。」

老闆：「玉琴呢？」

玉琴：「⋯⋯老闆，為什麼每年都只有我加班？」

老闆：「因為——玉琴顧粽。」

玉琴：「⋯⋯」

老闆：「欲擒故縱，懂嗎？」

玉琴：「我懂，所以我也要辭職。」

老闆：「⋯⋯」

玉琴：「叫做欲辭故走。」

隔壁同事們默默鼓掌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「玉琴顧粽」=「欲擒故縱」的天后級諧音梗，加上玉琴反殺「欲辭故走」——適合傳給每年端午都要加班的同事，或是傳到公司群組讓老闆看了會心一笑（也可能會心一驚）
variations: 短版：「老闆：玉琴顧粽。員工：欲辭故走，我懂。」
情侶版：「為什麼我每年端午都想你？因為——玉琴顧粽，我顧你。」

en.content:
Day before Dragon Boat Festival. Boss walks into the office: "Who's working tomorrow?"

Silence.

Boss: "Roll call then. Chen?"

Chen: "My family has zongzi waiting."

Boss: "Lee?"

Lee: "Grandma already wrapped them."

Boss: "Yuqin?"

Yuqin: "...Boss, why is it always me every year?"

Boss: "Because — Yuqin guards the zongzi." (sounds like 'play hard to get' in Mandarin)

Yuqin: "..."

Boss: "It's a pun. Get it?"

Yuqin: "I get it. So I quit."

Boss: "..."

Yuqin: "That's also a pun. 'Quit hard to leave.'"

The neighboring desks slow-clap.
en.editorNote: Built around a viral Taiwanese name-pun where 玉琴顧粽 (Yuqin guards zongzi) sounds identical to 欲擒故縱 (play hard to get). The employee's counter-pun seals it. Send to the colleague who always gets stuck with holiday shifts
variations: Short version: "Boss: Yuqin guards the zongzi. Employee: And I quit — that's also a pun."
Couple version: "Why do I miss you every Dragon Boat Festival? Because Yuqin guards the zongzi, and I guard you."

items.sourceNote: 「Karl Marx 留下難懂的書，屈原給我們三天連假」流傳中文網路的屈原 vs. 名人比較梗；改寫為原創學生對話

zh-tw.content:
歷史老師：「屈原對中華文化的貢獻是什麼？」

小明舉手：「給我們三天連假。」

老師：「⋯⋯還有呢？」

小明：「跟粽子。」

老師：「我是問他的文學作品。」

小明：「⋯⋯老師，我覺得屈原最厲害的作品就是《讓全國放三天》。」

老師：「那不是他寫的。」

小明：「但是是他用命爭取的。」

老師：「⋯⋯」

小明：「老師，孔子留下《論語》要我們背，孟子留下《孟子》要我們考，屈原留下三天連假——你說誰最愛學生？」

全班默默點頭。

老師：「⋯⋯下課。」

小明：「老師你看，連你都同意了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 屈原 vs. 孔孟的「連假貢獻論」——適合考完試的學生群組，或是傳給每年端午都在感謝屈原的朋友。重點在於最後老師「下課」的默認，讓笑點自己降落
variations: 短版：「孔子要我們背，屈原讓我們放——誰最愛學生？」
上班族版：「老闆：誰最愛員工？屈原。因為他是唯一讓我們放假的古人。」

en.content:
History teacher: "What did Qu Yuan contribute to Chinese culture?"

Xiaoming raises his hand: "A three-day weekend."

Teacher: "...And?"

Xiaoming: "Zongzi."

Teacher: "I meant his literary works."

Xiaoming: "...Sir, I think his greatest work is *Let The Entire Country Have Three Days Off*."

Teacher: "He didn't write that."

Xiaoming: "But he died for it."

Teacher: "..."

Xiaoming: "Confucius left us texts to memorize. Mencius left us texts for the exam. Qu Yuan left us a long weekend. You tell me who actually loved the students."

The entire class nods.

Teacher: "...Class dismissed."

Xiaoming: "See, even you agree."
en.editorNote: Plays on the viral comparison that Qu Yuan is the only ancient Chinese figure students truly thank — because his death gave them a public holiday. The teacher's silent surrender at the end is the real punchline. Works in school chats and office chats alike
variations: Short version: "Confucius made us memorize. Qu Yuan made us nap. Who really loved students?"
Office version: "Best ancient Chinese figure? Qu Yuan — the only one who got us a paid day off."

items.sourceNote: 端午廢話文學「用粽葉包手機延長續航」延伸格式；改寫為原創都市傳說對話

zh-tw.content:
「你知道端午節的隱藏功能嗎？」朋友神秘地說。

「什麼？」

「把手機用粽葉包起來蒸 60 分鐘，電池續航會延長兩倍。」

「⋯⋯真的假的？」

「真的。」

「為什麼？」

「因為手機就壞了，你買新的當然續航變兩倍。」

「⋯⋯」

「還有第二個。」

「我不想聽。」

「把你前任的照片用粽繩綁起來丟進龍舟河，可以斬斷孽緣。」

「⋯⋯然後呢？」

「然後你會被環保局罰六千。」

「⋯⋯」

「最後一個。」

「拜託不要。」

「端午節午時把粽子立起來，可以許願。」

「這個我聽過。」

「願望內容是『拜託這顆粽子不要倒』。」

「⋯⋯朋友，你今年是不是不打算過了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午限定廢話文學三連發——適合傳到朋友群組無聊鬥嘴用，重點在於「斬斷孽緣會被環保局罰款」這種反差，越廢越好笑；如果對方真的試了立粽，記得提醒他願望不要許得太大
variations: 短版：「粽葉包手機延長續航——因為手機壞了你要買新的。」
情話版：「想斬斷孽緣丟河裡？那妳怎麼還沒丟掉我？」

en.content:
"Did you know there's a hidden Dragon Boat Festival hack?" my friend says, lowering his voice.

"What?"

"Wrap your phone in bamboo leaves and steam it for sixty minutes. Battery life doubles."

"...Seriously?"

"Seriously."

"How?"

"Because your phone dies, so you buy a new one. New phone, double the battery."

"..."

"Second hack."

"I don't want it."

"Tie your ex's photo with zongzi string and throw it in the river during the dragon boat race. Cuts the karmic tie."

"...Then what?"

"Then the environmental agency fines you two hundred bucks."

"..."

"Last one."

"Please no."

"At noon on Dragon Boat Festival, stand a zongzi upright and make a wish."

"I've heard that."

"The wish is: 'please don't fall over, zongzi.'"

"...Buddy, are you okay this year."
en.editorNote: Trio of nonsense 'hacks' in the style of Taiwanese 廢話文學 (useless-truths humor). Send to a friend group chat — the karmic-tie-meets-environmental-fine is usually the one that lands. The 'stand a zongzi upright and wish' is a real Dragon Boat tradition, which makes the punchline funnier
variations: Short version: "Wrap your phone in bamboo leaves — battery doubles, because it dies and you buy a new one."
